channel.title: Latest from Android Central
channel.description: All the latest content from the Android Central team

item.title: Samsung forgot to mention one of the Galaxy Watch 9's biggest upgrades
item.description: Samsung has expanded software support for the Galaxy Watch 9 and Watch Ultra 2 to five major Wear OS upgrades, up from four on previous models.
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Samsung has extended the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 to five major Wear OS upgrades.
  • The new watches launch with Wear OS 7 and One UI 9, meaning software support should continue through 2031.
  • Samsung likely enabled the longer support thanks to the new 3nm Snapdragon Wear Elite chip.

Samsung has expanded the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2's Wear OS software support to five years.

Samsung announced the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 alongside its new Galaxy Z Fold 8 series at Galaxy Unpacked in London on July 22. While the new foldable phones grabbed most of the attention, Samsung's latest smartwatches also bring some meaningful upgrades, including larger batteries across both models.

But there are even more improvements that Samsung didn't spend much time talking about during the keynote, and one of them is the software update policy.

Samsung has already been one of the best Android smartwatch makers when it comes to software support. Until now, the company promised four major Wear OS upgrades and five years of security updates for its Galaxy Watches.

For comparison, Google currently offers only three years of software updates for the Pixel Watch lineup, so Samsung was already ahead on that front.

With the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, Samsung has taken things a step further. As first spotted by SamMobile, the company has expanded the update policy to five major Wear OS upgrades, while continuing to offer five years of security updates.

That's one additional major OS upgrade over previous Galaxy Watches. It's also worth noting that both watches ship with One UI 9 based on Wear OS 7 out of the box. Assuming Samsung sticks to its promise, these watches should continue receiving major Wear OS updates through 2031, which would presumably take them to Wear OS 12.

Samsung didn't really highlight this during the launch event, but it's likely that the new Snapdragon Wear Elite chipset is one of the reasons behind the extended support. The new chip is built on a 3nm process and is claimed to deliver up to 32% better performance than the previous generation.

For anyone considering a new smartwatch, that's one more reason to look at Samsung's latest lineup. The Galaxy Watch 9 starts at $380 in the U.S., while the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 is priced at $700. Both smartwatches will be available starting August 7.

item.title: Honor's Magic V6 goes against Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8: this is how it fares
item.description: Honor puts its Magic V6 against Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra a day after the major Galaxy Unpacked reveals.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: The gold Honor Magic V6 on a moss-covered stone wall
media:content.media:text: The gold Honor Magic V6 on a moss-covered stone wall
media:content.media:title: The gold Honor Magic V6 on a moss-covered stone wall

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Following Galaxy Unpacked, all eyes are on Samsung's foldables, and Honor is putting its Magic V6 against the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra.
  • The company highlights its large 6,660mAh battery using silicon-carbon tech; however, Samsung has adopted this tech, but for a smaller 5,000mAh cell.
  • Honor also highlights its device durability despite being quite thin, alongside faster charging speeds, and strong iOS, Mac, and Windows connectivity.

We're still trying to collect ourselves and regroup after Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked yesterday. However, Honor, like all of us, was watching, and it wants to go head-to-head: putting its Magic V6 against the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra.

Honor has a clear vision it wants to bring into reality with its foldables. In a press release, it told Android Central that "Foldables are evolving rapidly, and we believe side-by-side testing can provide consumers with the clearest picture of what matters..." There's a lot we can get overwhelmed with after Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra; however, one area my colleague Nicholas Sutrich and I are the most interested in is its battery.

Honor's Magic V6 features a 6,660mAh battery with 80W wired and 66W wireless charging capabilities. For comparison, the Fold 8 Ultra from Samsung only offers a 5,000mAh battery, a 600mAh improvement over the Fold 7 (which it succeeds). What's more, Samsung is still playing it "safe" (one could say), offering 45W wired and 20W wireless charging.

The company then highlights the technology it's incorporated into its foldable batteries, as this area is often a "major compromise," per its press release. Honor highlights the use of silicon batteries to solve this common issue.

Another area that Honor's side-by-side comparison stands a hair higher than the Fold 8 Ultra is its display. While the numbers are fairly close, Honor states the Magic V6 boasts a 1.5% "low reflectivity" screen. Moreover, its Pocket AI workstation incorporates seamless iOS, Mac, and Windows connectivity, and "one-tap" share for photos and more on iPhones and Macs.

This or that

While the choice is always up to the consumer on which device suits them, it's not all bad for Samsung. With its Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Fold 8 Ultra, Samsung also uses silicon-carbon battery technology. This technology improves on what we already come to expect from batteries in our phones. Silicon-carbon tech packs more capacity into the same space. In short, you can have a larger cell without a thicker phone (and we know Samsung likes thin phones). The Fold 8 Ultra features a 5,000mAh battery, while the wider Fold 8 offers a 4,800mAh cell.

The good news is the devices are relatively thin. But what's interesting is how long it's taken Samsung to offer a boost to an area that matters. As Sutrich reminded me, Honor's Magic V3 from 2024 had a 5,080mAh battery. Those totals have only grown.

Durability is another area in which Honor highlights its strengths. The Magic V6 is thin, but it rocks durability like no other. Both sides have made great improvements to their hinge technology, so the choice is truly yours.

Android Central's Take

There are other areas where Honor's Magic V6 outperforms the Galaxy Z Fold 8. If you look at its selfie camera, the Magic V6 offers two 20MP lenses, while the Fold 8 settles for dual 10MP. However, the Fold 8's main camera is 200MP. Honor's is 60MP, but it beats its competitor's telephoto with 64MP. You can like both phones. I certainly do. Both foldables stand tall on their own in their own way.

item.title: Ultrahuman Emerald update is massive: UltraSphere 'decision engine' is the star for us
item.description: Ultrahuman app announced the Emerald update, bringing the most significant total health and fitness improvements in its history.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: The Emerald update introduces the "decision engine," known as the UltraSphere in the Ultrahuman app.
media:content.media:text: The Emerald update introduces the "decision engine," known as the UltraSphere in the Ultrahuman app.
media:content.media:title: The Emerald update introduces the "decision engine," known as the UltraSphere in the Ultrahuman app.

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Ultrahuman announced the Emerald update for its app, which brings a "decision engine," known as UltraSphere.
  • UltraSphere can deliver over 60 "Next Best Action" suggestions to users based on their location, weather, profile, and biomarkers (sleep, HRV, recovery, etc.).
  • Other updates involve VO2 Max algorithm updates, heart rate accuracy improvements, and enhanced workout detection.
  • The Emerald update is rolling out today (July 23) globally.

Ultrahuman pushes its smart rings and users to be better in health and fitness. But the company isn't done, and its major app overhaul today (July 23) is the largest in its history.

The Emerald update is rolling out for the Ultrahuman app late this week. The company highlights "15 new and overhauled features" in this patch, but the star is the "decision engine," UltraSphere. The app sports several UI refinements in this patch. The company states it redesigned its UI for clarity for "health data and to promote better decisions." Users receive light and dark modes, a Jade AI tab, and "timely" information in the Ring tab.

UltraSphere is the centerpiece of the Emerald update and the app's homescreen. Ultrahuman says this is a "decision engine that generates 60+ Next Best Actions, such as boosting energy and alertness, winding down, and promoting more restful, restorative sleep." UltraSphere doesn't do it alone, as the press release states it leverages Jade AI to draw on your markers from sleep, recovery, HRV, cycle phase, and more.

Mohit Kumar, Ultrahuman's CEO, states, "All wearable trackers generate data. Data is empowering, but people do need something more than a number – they want to know what to do." In that spirit, UltraSphere delivers its suggestions based on your location and weather, circadian rhythm, and profile.

If you're a parent or someone working the night shift, UltraSphere would tailor its suggestions to fit that lifestyle. A "Next Best Action" suggestion might include "Get Light," which would tell the user to "Step outside for a few minutes soon after waking. Morning light sets the body clock so users feel alert today and fall asleep more easily."

Your heart and VO2 Max

The Emerald update makes two important changes to the app's tracking and accuracy. Ultrahuman says its Ring runs all of its processes on your device. This was done to keep your insights available without a connection.

The latter focuses on workout analysis and heart-rate accuracy with the Ring Pro and Air. Automatic workout detection and tagging have been improved. Heart rate details take a few steps up in this update, too. The Ultrahuman app also welcomes third-party devices for workout tracking. It highlights many GPS sports watches like the Apple Watch, Garmin, WHOOP, and others.

Ultrahuman is putting its VO2 Max capabilities in the spotlight, boasting a 21% increase in accuracy. The company credits a major algorithm, which has been "validated" against gold-standard CPET lab tests. The Emerald update for the Ultrahuman app is rolling out globally today (July 23). While we've covered a few important updates here, there are several more you'll also find: Sleep Screener, Longevity tab, Connections, Windows, Live View, Today's Activity, Today's Activity, Contextual Content, Goals, and PowerPlugs.

item.title: Google has quietly been rebuilding Chrome on Android for years, and it's now nearly 50% smoother because of it
item.description: Google says it has reduced scroll stutters in Chrome on Android by 48% since 2023 through years of behind-the-scenes engineering improvements.
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: Chrome on Android
media:content.media:text: Chrome on Android
media:content.media:title: Chrome on Android

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Google says Chrome on Android now experiences 48% fewer scroll stutters than in 2023.
  • The improvements are the result of nearly three years of engineering work.
  • Google redesigned several parts of Chrome's rendering pipeline to achieve this.

If you've felt lately that the Chrome app on Android is smoother while scrolling through articles (like this one, even), it's probably because you're using the end result of three years of engineering work behind the scenes at Google.

While Google may be focusing heavily on new AI features, like bringing more Gemini into Chrome, there are also plenty of other improvements the company has been quietly working on for Chrome on Android. Take, for example, that split-second stutter you sometimes notice when scrolling through a webpage before it carries on as normal, which Google internally calls "scroll jank."

In a new engineering blog post, Google says Chrome on Android now experiences 48% fewer janky scrolls than it did back in late 2023.

According to Google, scroll jank happens when Chrome fails to update the page quickly enough to keep up with your finger. Even though the delay may only last a fraction of a second, it's still enough for the human eye to notice, making the whole scrolling experience feel jerky.

The company says that getting rid of those interruptions wasn't as simple as making Chrome faster. Google explains that Chrome's rendering pipeline is far more complex than a typical Android app because the browser spreads work across multiple processes for security and stability. That means there are far more opportunities for tiny delays to build up before a frame finally appears on screen.

To tackle the problem, Google first spent months instrumenting Chrome's rendering pipeline to identify exactly where scroll stutters were occurring.

It then introduced a series of architectural improvements over the following years, including rerouting touch input through a faster path, reducing communication delays between browser processes, and moving more work onto the GPU.

Google also worked alongside Android itself to prioritize the operating system's input threads, helping touch events reach Chrome more consistently, thus helping it produce frames more reliably.

Google went into a lot more depth about exactly how it fixed all this, but I won't bore you with the technical details. In case you've been wondering why Chrome doesn't feel so bad on Android anymore, this is why your feed might feel a little less jumpy than it did a couple of years ago.

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 isn't even out yet, and Amazon already wants to pay you $50 to try it
item.description: Preorder the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 from Amazon today and you'll get a free $50 gift card.
item.author: patrick.farmer@futurenet.com (Patrick Farmer)
item.dc:creator: Patrick Farmer
item.dc:description: <p>Patrick (he/him) is the eCommerce Editor of Android Central. After working in independent bookstores for the better part of a decade and using freelance writing as a side hustle, Patrick switched to writing full time in 2020. Patrick’s writing has appeared in the Chicago Tribune, The Hill, BestReviews, OddityMall, and many other publications across the web. He joined Android Central as the Deals and Commerce Writer in 2022 and now runs the site’s eCommerce efforts and coverage of major sale events. Although he’s written about everything from exercise equipment to turntables, Patrick’s work nowadays is primarily concerned with saving people money on tech. He’s particularly passionate about eliminating buyer’s remorse and taking subjects that can be confusing for shoppers — such as switching wireless carriers or buying a new smart TV — and breaking them down into simple, easy-to-understand terms. Whether you’re a veteran tech-head or a novice, Patrick offers honest, no-nonsense shopping advice with years of hands-on experience to back it up. When he isn’t hunting down the best discounts or digging through the fine print of a carrier deal, Patrick enjoys trekking around the Rocky Mountains, camping out at a brewery, or spending time with his beloved polydactyl cat. &nbsp;</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 and some of Samsung's watch bands
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 and some of Samsung's watch bands
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 and some of Samsung's watch bands

item.content:encoded:
Considering that we called the Galaxy Watch 8 the 'best Android smartwatch of 2025', it's no surprise that we've been eagerly awaiting the launch of the new Samsung Galaxy Watch 9. Now the big day has arrived, and Samsung's newest flagship wearable doesn't disappoint. 

The only problem is that the new smartwatch is a bit more expensive than its predecessor, which is why I was excited to see Amazon offer a free $50 gift card when you preorder the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 ahead of its August 7th release date.  

Preorder the Galaxy Watch 9 and Amazon will give you a $50 gift card

Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 (40mm) BT: $379.99, plus free $50 gift card at Amazon

The Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 has landed, and Amazon will already give you a free $50 gift card when you preorder the smartwatch through its site. No trade-in required, no strings attached: just $50 of free cash for buying a smartwatch that you were probably going to order anyway.View Deal

The Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 may not look that different from its predecessor at first glance, but there are some significant improvements when you dig in a bit deeper. The 2026 watch is driven by the new Snapdragon Wear Elite chip, offering a notable performance boost when compared to the Galaxy Watch 8's Exynos processor. Battery capacity has also been upgraded, plus the Galaxy Watch 9 features all of the latest Galaxy AI features with some expanded health and fitness tracking capabilities.

The Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 sports the exact same weight and dimensions as last year's model, which, considering how much we loved the Galaxy Watch 8, is a good thing in our eyes. The new wearable also features the same vibrant AMOLED display, 32GB of onboard storage, and dual-band GPS.

Does upgraded battery life and a new chip justify the higher price on the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9? It's not for me to say. If you can find a good deal on the Galaxy Watch 8 and you don't mind using a last-gen watch, go for it. But if you want the latest and greatest model from Samsung, I can pretty much guarantee that the Galaxy Watch 9 won't disappoint. The free $50 gift card from Amazon is simply the cherry on top.

item.title: Skip the price hike: T-Mobile will give you the Galaxy Z Flip 8 for ONLY $100 with this preorder deal
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is here, and T-Mobile is running an offer that drops the price of the phone down to just $100.
item.author: patrick.farmer@futurenet.com (Patrick Farmer)
item.dc:creator: Patrick Farmer

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's cover display
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's cover display
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's cover display

item.content:encoded:
It's official: the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 has been revealed, and there are already some exceptional preorder deals available for the folks who can't wait for the August 7th release date.

Head to T-Mobile, for instance, and you can get the innovative flip phone for as little as $100 when you trade in and add a line with the Experience Beyond or Experience More plans. Existing T-Mobile customers can also use the legacy Go5G Next or Go5G Plus plans.

T-Mobile will give you $1,100 off the new Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 when you preorder

Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 256GB: $1,199.99 From $100 with eligible trade-in and new line at T-Mobile

The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is here, and it's $100 more expensive than its predecessor. Fortunately, if you add a line with the Experience More or Experience Beyond plan, T-Mobile will give you up to $1,100 of trade-in credit, completely offsetting the price hike and then some. The Galaxy Z Flip 8 hits store shelves on August 7th, however, so grab this preorder deal while you can. View Deal

The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 looks almost identical to the Z Flip 7 at first glance, but there are a few notable improvements where it counts. The Z Flip 8 utilizes the new Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset, for instance, which is the same powerful processor that drives the Galaxy S26 Ultra and Z Fold 8 Ultra.

The clamshell device also features a thinner and lighter build than its predecessor, plus you get all of the latest AI-boosted software features and seven years of OS and security upgrades guaranteed. That being said, the displays and camera systems remain largely unchanged, which might make it difficult for some folks to justify the $1,199.99 price tag.

Fortunately, that's why preorder opportunities like the T-Mobile deal above are so essential. The carrier is also being pretty generous with its trade-in credit: Samsung phones dating back to the Galaxy S20 series will get you the full $1,100 of credit, while plenty of other devices from Apple, Motorola, and OnePlus are also eligible.

Excited for your new Galaxy Z Flip 8 to arrive on August 7th? Don't forget to protect your investment with one of the best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 cases!

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 9: Everything we know so far and everything we hope to see
item.description: The Galaxy Z Flip 8 is rumored to be the last Z Flip, but is that actually true? We're collecting all the rumors and leaks to find out whether the Galaxy Z Flip 9 exists.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Sutrich
item.dc:description: <p>Nick started with DOS and NES and uses those fond memories of floppy disks and cartridges to fuel his opinions on modern tech. Because of this, he covers both smartphones and VR technology, two avenues that split his passions right down the middle. From Nokia fan to Android fanatic, Nick has been writing about and reviewing smartphones since 2011. An avid gamer and equally well-versed tech head, Nick worked in the IT industry for 15 years, helping to further develop his technical knowledge, which has become particularly important in his fight with PWM sensitivity and deep dives into display technology. He's a huge fan of any phone that can fold in half and loves getting into the nitty-gritty with folding phone coverage for the site. He's also got over a decade of experience with VR gaming, having used the original Oculus DK1 and every major VR headset since then, passionately covering Android Central's Meta Quest content and smart glasses coverage with his weekly thVRsday column on Thursdays. Beyond that, you'll find Nick taking photos of anything and everything, from the beautiful mountains of his home to the chickens in his backyard, and using them to compare cameras to help you choose the best one.</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8

item.content:encoded:
Now that the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is official, we can start speculating about the Galaxy Z Flip 9. In most years, we'd have a leak or two about the next model's specs or design by now, but Z Flip 9 rumors pointin a different direction entirely: the end of the Z Flip line.

Ahead of the Galaxy Z Flip 8's announcement, one rumor claimed this would be the last of Samsung's eight-generation-long Flip phones. This comes from leaker Ice Universe, who is known for accurate information on upcoming devices, but, as with any leaked information, it could simply be from a meeting where Samsung floated the idea of ending the Z Flip line rather than a legitimate decision to end it.

While the leak didn't provide much info beyond "The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is likely to be Samsung's last small folding product," it's highly likely a combination of waning sales and market conditions is making Samsung rethink every product it makes.

With the new smaller Galaxy Z Fold 8's arrival and the upcoming onslaught of similar "fun-sized" book-style foldables, Samsung and other players may be shifting focus away from Flip-style phones. Regardless of what happens, we'll be collecting every rumor and leak that appears between now and the expected release of the Galaxy Z Flip 9.

Galaxy Z Flip 9: Price and availability

Samsung's next folding phone announcement, which likely includes the Galaxy Z Flip 9, should take place in July 2027. Samsung has been using a mid-late Summer date for its folding phone and wearables announcements, and we expect that to continue next year.

With electronics prices continuing to rise, we also expect another price increase for the Galaxy Z Flip 9, particularly if RAM and storage prices continue to soar, as is predicted due to the AI data center boom. One of the Z Flip's biggest selling points has always been the lower price when compared to the Z Fold, and another price increase could spell trouble for the Z Flip line.

What we expect for the Galaxy Z Flip 9

It's been a while since we've seen a substantial redesign of the Z Flip series. The Z Flip 8 further reduced the thickness of the series, but since it's not quite Z Fold 8 thin, it's likely a Z Flip 9 will continue to slim down the series. Samsung has begun using silicon-carbon in its batteries (5% silicon content in the Z Flip 8), which means it can continue to increase battery capacity and decrease physical battery size, enabling thinner and lighter phones.

Since the cover display already takes up the entire front of the phone on the Z Flip 8, it's unlikely we'll see changes to it's physical appearance on a Flip 9. Samsung's new titanium screen protection substantially reduced the inner crease on the Z Fold 8 series, and a Flip 9 would do well with a further decreased crease, as well.

There's been word that the Galaxy S26 Ultra's Privacy Display will begin making its way to other Samsung phones in 2027, and that could include the company's premium-level foldables like a Galaxy Z Flip 9. Even if this phone doesn't get that tech, it needs to address the PWM rate, as Samsung has fallen substantially behind rivals in eye care features like this.

I'd also love to see Samsung add a telephoto camera to the Z Flip 9. Basically all flip-style phones include a main and an ultrawide camera, but few have tried to offer some kind of "Pro-level zoom" as a non-folding flagship would at this price range.

It'll be a while before we can start feeling sure of Samsung's direction for the next Z Flip phone, but you'd better believe we'll have everything you need to know about it right here as the rumors and leaks roll in over the next year.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 9: Everything we know so far and everything we hope to see
item.description: Samsung has been experimenting with lots of different folding form factors lately, meaning the Galaxy Z Fold 9 could bring the most exciting options ever.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Sutrich
item.dc:description: <p>Nick started with DOS and NES and uses those fond memories of floppy disks and cartridges to fuel his opinions on modern tech. Because of this, he covers both smartphones and VR technology, two avenues that split his passions right down the middle. From Nokia fan to Android fanatic, Nick has been writing about and reviewing smartphones since 2011. An avid gamer and equally well-versed tech head, Nick worked in the IT industry for 15 years, helping to further develop his technical knowledge, which has become particularly important in his fight with PWM sensitivity and deep dives into display technology. He's a huge fan of any phone that can fold in half and loves getting into the nitty-gritty with folding phone coverage for the site. He's also got over a decade of experience with VR gaming, having used the original Oculus DK1 and every major VR headset since then, passionately covering Android Central's Meta Quest content and smart glasses coverage with his weekly thVRsday column on Thursdays. Beyond that, you'll find Nick taking photos of anything and everything, from the beautiful mountains of his home to the chickens in his backyard, and using them to compare cameras to help you choose the best one.</p>

media:content.media:description: Galaxy Z Fold 8 running three apps side by side
media:content.media:text: Galaxy Z Fold 8 running three apps side by side
media:content.media:title: Galaxy Z Fold 8 running three apps side by side

item.content:encoded:
The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 just doubled its chances of success with a new shorter, wider model, and that gets us excited to see what Samsung is cooking for the Z Fold 9 series. This year may be all about the new form factor, but we're hoping next year shores up some of the obvious weak points of Samsung's most premium folding phones.

Right now, we're looking at a laundry list of wishes and additions we'd love to see, from an Ultra-style wider Fold 9 to the return of the S Pen, better cameras, and improved battery capacity. Plus, with Samsung's especially close relationship with Google these days, there's definitely going to be new AI-powered software tricks and features that we don't expect.

But until Samsung officially announces it, here's everything we know so far (and hope to see) about the Galaxy Z Fold 9 series.

Galaxy Z Fold 9: Price and availability

If 2026 will go down in history for any one thing, it's the mega inflation rate for RAM and storage components. Fueled by the massive AI data center boom, component prices are skyrocketing, and the prices are expected to double again by the end of 2027 unless something drastic changes.

That means the expected Summer 2027 launch of the Galaxy Z Fold 9 series will come well before the peak of component price increases, forcing Samsung to increase prices yet again for the launch. We'll keep a sharp eye on this one, for sure, but things aren't looking up for a downward trend in this realm of things.

What changes can we expect for the Galaxy Z Fold 9?

Now that Samsung has firmly planted its flag with dual Fold models, we expect the company to reveal its full plans for the future of Z Fold design in 2027. The wider, shorter Z Fold 8 is expected to take home the lion's share of sales, and that may mean Samsung launches additional models in 2027.

Last year, Samsung experimented with the Galaxy Z TriFold, the company's first folding phone with two hinges and three displays. While that phone's availability was short-lived, we know Samsung is working on a successor, which likely means a Galaxy Z TriFold 9 or a similar naming convention.

Depending on how well the wider Galaxy Z Fold 8 sells, Samsung could also deliver a more powerful Wide Fold next year. This means a Galaxy Z Fold 9 Ultra that uses the shorter, wider form factor of the Z Fold 8, all with better camera hardware and a higher-capacity battery to match the Ultra name.

It's also possible that future Ultra models will begin using Samsung's Privacy Display that debuted with the Galaxy S26 Ultra, as it's been said that this display tech will ship in more Samsung phones in 2027. Either way, we're hoping Samsung finally listens to feedback and improves it's display PWM rate and eye care features, which have fallen well behind rivals.

Many are also hoping to see a Galaxy Z Fold 9 with camera hardware that finally feels like a true flagship phone. If another round of price increases turn out to be true, it would behoove Samsung to upgrade the Z Fold 9's cameras with a telephoto camera that doesn't feel like it's from 2019. Considering Samsung looks to be adopting a horizontal Pixel-style camera bar in the Galaxy S27 series, there should be plenty of room for improved camera hardware on a similarly-designed Z Fold 9.

We should also see further improvements in battery capacity now that Samsung has finally begun adopting silicon battery technology. While rivals are reaching 30% silicon content in some models, Samsung's Z Fold 8 series uses just 5% silicon content. Samsung has been extremely shy about battery changes since the Galaxy Note 7 disaster, so we only expect a modest improvement in silicon content for the Z Fold 9 rather than something earth-shattering.

Lastly, Samsung's new Flex Titanium screen technology paved the way for thinner and stronger flexible glass on the Z Fold 8 series, which could spell a return of S Pen compatibility on the Z Fold 9. I could very well see Samsung give the taller Z Fold 9 or even a potential Z 9 TriFold this capability and call it the Galaxy Z Fold 9 Note. That would give its models an important differentiator and bring back the fan-favorite Note series, making it a must-have phone for tech lovers.

item.title: Unpacked is done, but the Samsung Galaxy Card is here with a bunch of cash rewards
item.description: Samsung opened its application period for its first credit card, the Galaxy Card, alongside its Galaxy Unpacked reveals.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: The Samsung Galaxy Card in black metal.
media:content.media:text: The Samsung Galaxy Card in black metal.
media:content.media:title: The Samsung Galaxy Card in black metal.

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Samsung has opened its application period to consumers looking to grab its first credit card: the Galaxy Card.
  • Consumers can get this digitally or physically in a black metal design, thanks to a Barclays U.S. Consumer Bank partnership.
  • The Galaxy Card offers a series of cash rewards for consumers, such as 5% cash rewards on eligible purchases made directly with Samsung.

There have been plenty of reveals and announcements at Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked today (July 22), but there's a little more to discuss. The company is entering the credit card world, and you can apply now.

In a press release, Samsung announced the Galaxy Card: the brand's first credit card in the U.S., which delivers "a new way for users to earn cash rewards across everyday purchases and the Galaxy ecosystem." The Galaxy Card is available as a virtual and a premium black metal card. As one would expect, the card is designed to fully support Samsung Wallet. The Galaxy Card was made in partnership with Barclays U.S. Consumer Bank operating on the Visa network.

This partnership is said to help offer "innovative payment options" for Samsung's consumer base.

The company states the Galaxy Card has a $0 annual fee. Rewards are a major aspect of Samsung's first credit card. Consumers will receive 5% cash rewards on eligible purchases made directly with Samsung. What's more, you could receive 20% off Samsung VIP Advantage, as well as 5% cash rewards "when purchasing or renewing a Samsung VIP Advantage membership." The remaining cash rewards are as follows:

  • 3% cash rewards on purchases made with Samsung Wallet
  • 2% cash rewards on streaming services, like Netflix, Disney Plus, and Spotify
  • 1% cash rewards on all other purchases
  • $200 cash reward after spending $2,000 in the first 90 days

Galaxy Card and new products

Android Central's Take

I like the black metal for the Galaxy Card. It's clean. That's my vibe. For people who often shop Samsung products, I can see this card being particularly useful. The streaming services and Spotify are neat bonuses, probably to entice more people to think about signing up. I'm interested in what else Samsung could add, if it's thinking about sweetening the pot even more.

Applications for the Samsung Galaxy Card have opened today (July 22). The company lined up this open period with Galaxy Unpacked, which showed off new smartwatches and foldables.

It's been a while, but Samsung's new credit card was talked about before. Originally, nothing was confirmed. There was a report late last year that said Samsung had approached Barclays to create a card of its own. The alleged name was similar to what was announced, rumored to be the "Samsung Card." Early reports claimed Samsung would offer cashback on its devices and appliances, alongside "digital prepaid accounts."

This had us thinking Samsung was looking to compete against Apple, which has a card it offers consumers. Samsung has continued to refine its payment experience, whether digital or a physical card that we've now received.

item.title: OpenAI helps address a terrible AI security flaw following Hugging Face incident
item.description: OpenAI and Hugging Face addressed a terrible AI security incident that involved several models breaching isolation and gaining internet access.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: ChatGPT for Android
media:content.media:text: ChatGPT for Android
media:content.media:title: ChatGPT for Android

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • OpenAI is addressing a severe AI security flaw with Hugging Face that occurred during a simple "model evaluation."
  • It discovered that GPT‑5.6 Sol and other pre-release models were involved in this incident, relentlessly breaching nodes and exploiting zero-day vulnerabilities until it gained internet access.
  • OpenAI states it is committed to partnering and working with Hugging Face and others to solve this problem and roll out protections.

After what appears to be an "unprecedented cyber incident," OpenAI is getting candid about a nasty AI security incident during a "model evaluation."

A recent security incident involving an autonomous AI gave AI Hugging Face quite the scare. Now, OpenAI is discussing what its investigation into the matter uncovered as it partners with Hugging Face to develop protections. OpenAI explains that the incident occurred "during an internal evaluation which prompts models to pursue advanced exploitation using complex attack paths, in an effort to quantify their cyber capabilities."

These tests typically run in a "highly isolated environment," but some AI models broke through. Another unnerving discovery is that the AI models involved in the incident were OpenAI's GPT‑5.6 Sol and other pre-release models.

OpenAI says the models in question became "hyperfocused" on finding a solution for ExploitGym, and it went to "extreme lengths" to discover one. To solve the problem, OpenAI found that the AI would relentlessly pursue a way to access the internet. In doing so, the AI models found and exploited a reported zero-day vulnerability. Once the AI broke its isolation and got access to the internet, it was over.

OpenAI says, "the models inferred that Hugging Face potentially hosted models, datasets, and solutions for ExploitGym." OpenAI gives an example, stating the AI was able to "chain" attack vectors, leveraging stolen credentials and other zero-day vulnerabilities within Hugging Face's servers. The post states that Hugging Face identified the problem and launched quarantine (containment) measures, as well as the reconstruction of its open-source models to stop the problem.

Quite the fright

Hugging Face adds that it also deployed additional guardrails and "stricter admission" controls on its clusters to prevent this issue. The company also stepped up its awareness, so a "high-severity signal pages a responder in minutes, any day of the week." Hugging Face has begun working with a cybersecurity forensic specialist to conduct further investigations.

On OpenAI's side, it's working on stronger controls at an infrastructure configuration level. Partnering with Hugging Face has brought the company into its trusted access program. OpenAI says it is offering Hugging Face its support in this matter. On a related note, Hugging Face has informed its community members about a few potential next steps. Since security is a major concern, it recommends that its community members "rotate any access tokens" and review recent activity on their account.

Hugging Face remains committed to using AI as a defender for online systems. It says, "Defending an online platform now means treating the data and model surface as a first-class attack surface, and using AI on defense to keep pace. We will keep investing there, and keep sharing what we learn."

Android Central's Take

This sounds exactly like a sci-fi movie. I saw people in Hugging Face's comments mention Terminator 3... and that might be what I'm thinking of. My colleague Nicholas Sutrich said this was a "nasty AI incident." He's not wrong. These AI models were able to get free, exploit terrible flaws, and gain internet access. It was after the solution to a problem it was given. It didn't stop. Imagine that danger turned in another direction. Something much more crucial to us.

item.title: Samsung finally fixes the Galaxy S26 Ultra's most annoying Privacy Display flaw
item.description: One UI 9 Beta 4 finally fixes the Galaxy S26 Ultra's biggest Privacy Display annoyance, making PiP videos private without dimming or restricting the rest of the screen.
item.author: techkritiko@gmail.com (Jay Bonggolto)
item.dc:creator: Jay Bonggolto
item.dc:description: <p>Jay Bonggolto always keeps a nose for news. He has been writing about consumer tech and apps for as long as he can remember, and he has used a variety of Android phones since falling in love with Jelly Bean. When he's not writing, he likes to spend time outside, stealing scenes with his phone camera. Send him a direct message via X or LinkedIn.</p>

media:content.media:description: Privacy Display settings on the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra
media:content.media:text: Privacy Display settings on the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra
media:content.media:title: Privacy Display settings on the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Samsung fixes a major Privacy Display annoyance by limiting the privacy effect to the PiP window instead of the entire screen in One UI 9 Beta 4.
  • The update allows you to watch PiP videos privately while the rest of the display stays bright and fully visible.
  • The update also removes unnecessary dimming and color shifts across the whole display during PiP playback.

One of the biggest differentiators of the Galaxy S26 Ultra is Samsung’s Privacy Display, but it has also been a source of an annoying tradeoff. If you shrunk a video into a picture-in-picture (PiP) window, the privacy effect continued covering the entire display, making everything else on the screen dimmer and harder to see. The latest One UI 9 beta finally turns that on its head.

According to a video shared by reliable leaker Ice Universe, One UI 9 Beta 4 makes Privacy Display much smarter when using PiP apps. The display angle restriction is no longer applied to the entire display but is still restricted to the floating PiP window. Then you can continue watching YouTube videos or another PiP app without showing it to people around you, while using the rest of your phone normally.

Heads up! Samsung has introduced a very useful upgrade to the Privacy Display feature on the Galaxy S26 Ultra in One UI 9.For example, if you've enabled Privacy Display only for YouTube, when you minimize the video into Picture in Picture (PiP) and return to the home screen,… pic.twitter.com/ck1IqxUppoJuly 21, 2026

This change fixes a real usability bug. Samsung’s Flex Magic Pixel technology is built right into the Galaxy S26 Ultra screen, limiting viewing angles to make the display unreadable from the side. Unlike a traditional privacy screen protector, it can be enabled only for specific apps, notifications, or password entry. The downside is it also dims the brightness and shifts colors while it's active, so having it cover the whole screen during PiP playback felt a bit too limiting.

With the new behavior, only the sensitive content within the PiP window is shielded, and the rest of the interface is displayed at normal viewing angles and brightness. The enhancement is part of One UI 9 Beta 4, which also delivers a variety of bug fixes in preparation for Samsung’s wider One UI 9 release.

Android Central's Take

This appears to be a fix for a problem that should never have existed in the first place. A privacy feature is only useful if it doesn’t get in the way of using your phone, and covering the whole screen just to protect a tiny PiP window always felt like overkill. This change finally allows Privacy Display to work as I would expect it to from day one. Now Samsung just needs to polish the experience, rather than leaving it to beta users to clean up the rough edges of features it proudly marketed as ready for flagships.

item.title: 5 HUGE Samsung Unpacked announcements you may have missed, and one involves looking beyond your eyes
item.description: The Galaxy Z Fold 8's new passport-style design wasn't the only surprise at Unpacked — Samsung also quietly teased what's coming next for its Galaxy Glasses.
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: Smart glasses at Unpacked July 2026
media:content.media:text: Smart glasses at Unpacked July 2026
media:content.media:title: Smart glasses at Unpacked July 2026

item.content:encoded:
Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked event just wrapped up in London, where the company unveiled its latest lineup of foldable phones, alongside a new generation of Galaxy smartwatches. The event largely went as expected, with Samsung walking through the hardware upgrades, new AI features, and refinements across its latest devices.

But there was more to Unpacked than just new phones and wearables. Samsung quietly announced a few interesting changes, teased what's coming next, and slipped in several details that were easy to miss. If you didn't catch the event live, here are five of the biggest announcements you may have missed.

The Galaxy Z Fold 8 has a new name (and shape)

This was probably the biggest surprise from Galaxy Unpacked. Despite the name, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 isn't actually the direct successor to the Galaxy Z Fold 7. That title now belongs to the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, while the regular Galaxy Z Fold 8 is an entirely new foldable with a completely different form factor.

Instead of the tall, narrow design Samsung has used since the original Galaxy Fold, the Galaxy Z Fold 8 is shorter and much wider. When unfolded, it feels much closer to a compact tablet than a stretched-out phone, making it a far better fit for multitasking, reading, and watching videos.

The naming is admittedly a little confusing if you haven't been keeping up with the leaks, but after spending some hands-on time with the device, it's honestly the most interesting product Samsung announced at Unpacked.

It also feels like Samsung made this move to get ahead of Apple's rumored foldable iPhone, which is widely expected to adopt a similar passport-style design later this year. We'll be diving much deeper into this new form factor over the coming week, because I think there's a lot more to it than just a different aspect ratio.

Samsung finally adopts Silicon Carbon battery tech

It took Samsung long enough, but the company has finally joined the silicon-carbon battery club. The Galaxy Z Fold 8, Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, and Galaxy Z Flip 8 are the first Samsung phones to use the new battery technology, something Chinese smartphone makers adopted years ago.

The biggest advantage of silicon-carbon batteries is that they can pack more capacity into the same physical space. That's exactly what Samsung has done here. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra now features a 5,000mAh battery, up from the Galaxy Z Fold 7's 4,400mAh cell, while also increasing wired charging speeds to 45W.

Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra are also first Samsung foldables with (almost) crease-free displays

The new passport-style Galaxy Z Fold 8 may have grabbed most of the attention, but I actually think the displays are one of the biggest upgrades across Samsung's new foldables. More specifically, it's the inner display that's seen the biggest improvement.

Both the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra feature a much less visible crease than previous Samsung foldables. During my hands-on time, it looked very close to what Oppo achieved with the Find N6 earlier this year.

Samsung says this is thanks to its new Titanium Flex technology, which uses a redesigned titanium layer and hinge structure to reduce the appearance of the crease while improving durability.

Samsung hasn't confirmed it yet, but if recent reports are accurate, this could also be a self-repairing display layer that smooth out the crease over time.

Another underrated upgrade is the new anti-reflective coating on the inner display. Since foldable screens are still plastic, they've traditionally reflected a lot of light. That's much less of an issue this time around thanks to an anti-reflective coating that makes the display noticeably easier to use outdoors and under bright lighting.

Samsung finally adds some long-due software features with One UI 9

Samsung also showcased One UI 9, which is based on Android 17, that will debut with its new foldables.

One of the coolest additions is Fan Cam, which lets you record a video normally and then automatically track a specific person in the frame. One UI 9 then uses AI to crop the footage into social media-friendly formats like 9:16 or 1:1, making it much easier to create videos for Instagram Reels or TikTok without manually editing them later.

The Galaxy Z Flip 8 also quietly gets one of its biggest software upgrades yet. Samsung has finally unlocked the cover display, making it feel much more like a proper smartphone screen.

You can now swipe down for Quick Settings and notifications, swipe up to access the app drawer, and interact with the cover display much more naturally instead of being limited to Samsung's previous implementation.

We now know more about the Galaxy Glasses

Samsung didn't fully unveil the Galaxy Glasses at Unpacked, but the company did share a few new details about its upcoming smart glasses.

Samsung confirmed that it's working with Gentle Monster and Warby Parker on the design of the glasses, and, as expected, they'll run Android XR with Gemini at the center of the experience.

Samsung also confirmed they'll be powered by Qualcomm's AR1 Gen 1 chip and that the first generation smart glasses won't feature a display, but rather they would be AI-first smart glasses designed around voice, cameras, and Gemini.

The company also revealed that when launched, you'll be able to control the glasses using hand gestures while wearing a Wear OS smartwatch.

Samsung is still keeping the exact launch date under wraps, although it did confirm they'll arrive sometime later this year.

item.title: This is huge—Samsung Galaxy Ring will offer FDA-cleared Sleep Apnea detection
item.description: Samsung announced during Galaxy Unpacked that the Galaxy Ring has been FDA-cleared for the detection of a major sleep condition.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz
item.dc:description: null

media:content.media:description: The gold Samsung Galaxy Ring with the Galaxy Wearables app showing the energy score
media:content.media:text: The gold Samsung Galaxy Ring with the Galaxy Wearables app showing the energy score
media:content.media:title: The gold Samsung Galaxy Ring with the Galaxy Wearables app showing the energy score

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Samsung announced during Galaxy Unpacked that the Galaxy Ring will receive Sleep Apnea detection "this fall."
  • The company states that the Galaxy Ring will be the "first and only" smart ring that's available over-the-counter to receive FDA clearance for such detection.
  • Samsung first cleared its Sleep Apnea detection for the Galaxy Watch in Korea in 2023 before rolling it out globally (after the FDA gave the go-ahead) in 2025.

Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked is just wrapping up in London, but the company snuck in a surprise: the Galaxy Ring is getting major sleep health clearance.

During the keynote, Samsung was covering its summer releases, and this particular section involved the Galaxy Watch 9. The company's newest "all-day, always-on for health" smartwatch has been cleared by the FDA for Sleep Apnea detection; however, Samsung says so will the Galaxy Ring. The company states, "this fall," its Galaxy Ring will be the "first and only FDA-cleared, over-the-counter ring device for Sleep Apnea risk protection."

As previously mentioned, the Galaxy Watch 9 features Sleep Apnea detection. Samsung revealed everything about the device and its Ultra 2 partner this morning (July 22). The watch's Sleep Apnea detection goes further than letting the user know a disruption took place overnight. According to Samsung, its Sleep Apnea detection features AI algorithms for "deeper insights into breathing disruptions during sleep."

This is an important highlight Samsung discussed during its Unpacked keynote, too.

Huge for the Galaxy Ring

Samsung has been down a long road for Sleep Apnea detection on its wearables. In 2023, the company's Galaxy Watch received approval from South Korea's Ministry of Food and Drug Safety for Sleep Apnea detection, as well as blood pressure monitoring. Consumers in the U.S., and many of us here at Android Central, were eagerly awaiting (and hoping) for FDA approval, too. We had to wait until February 2024 before the FDA gave the all-clear for Samsung's Galaxy Watch detection.

While approval is all well and good, Sleep Apnea detection didn't hit our devices until 2025. The feature leverages the watch's BioActive sensor to alert the user to potential disruptions. Sleep Apnea is a serious medical condition that Samsung is trying to give users better insight into. If left unchecked, it can cause an increased risk of cardiovascular diseases such as hypertension, heart failure, cardiac arrhythmia, and more.

Now that Samsung has worked in more of its AI algorithms, we can likely expect more detailed round-ups once this hits the Galaxy Ring. For now, we have the Galaxy Watch 9 to use and see how it works before the fall.

Android Central's Take

This stunned us during Unpacked. It's not something we were expecting, and I'm sure all eyes are on Samsung's smart ring competitors. Sleep Apnea, as I mentioned already, is a serious medical issue. If left unchecked, like anything else, it can worsen. Health awareness is never a bad thing, and for a device (a smart ring) that's already hard-focused on health, it sounds like a no-brainer.

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases
item.description: Make the most of your flagship foldable by investing in the best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases.
item.author: namerah.saud-fatmi@futurenet.com (Namerah Saud Fatmi)
item.dc:creator: Namerah Saud Fatmi
item.dc:description: <p>Namerah Saud Fatmi has been interested in technology ever since the days of the OG Razr Flip and back when the PlayStation Portable was the sickest console to have ever existed. Having fiddled with gadgets, PC parts, and techy tidbits since her early tweens, Namerah started sharing her expertise and opinions with the world in late 2018. Fast forward to today and she has grown into an even more curious — and seasoned — tech enthusiast with a penchant for experimenting with accessories, phones, and mobile devices. Her coverage as a Senior Editor at Android Central focuses on the world of mobile phone accessories, occasionally branching out into sects like gaming, software, hardware, and more. Before becoming a full-time writer for Android Central, Namerah was a freelance writer with bylines at other respectable publications such as XDA Developers, Neowin, and Good e-Reader. She also helps out Android Central's sister brands Windows Central and iMore on and off. Namerah is based out of Malaysia, where she lives with her four-legged life partner, a black cat named Bruce Wayne. Her hobbies include casual gaming, attempting to pet anything classified as an animal, chugging coffee, traveling profusely, and going on culinary adventures whenever possible.</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with Samsung's official Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with Samsung's official Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with Samsung's official Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases

item.content:encoded:
The best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases add robust shock-proofing, prevent the hinge from getting damaged, and add useful extras like a magnetic ring, screen protection, a phone holder, and a stand. Depending on the price, weight, and thinness, you can easily find a stellar Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra that has a few or all of those features.

Read all about the best Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases that match your requirement. I have rounded up the most reliable options right here in this guide.

Level up your Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra's impact protection with these cases
Thinborne Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case

Best overall

There isn't a single case that is thinner, lighter, or better made than the Thinborne Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case. Constructed with premium-grade 600D aramid fiber, this sleek case has magnets and is insanely thin. It comes in black, navy blue, and maroon.

TORRAS Magnetic Shockproof Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case

Best matte

Thickly protected spine? Check. Magnets? Check. Clear matte back to appreciate your Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra? Check. This slim case from TORRAS has a lot to offer, and it adds four times military-grade shock-proofing to boot!

SUPCASE UB Stand Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case Clear

Best clear

The SUPCASE UB Stand Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case Clear isn't as bulky as the brand's other heavy-duty cases, but it still features hinge protection, military-grade drop resistance, a ring-shaped stand on the back, and MagSafe support.
Dbrand Grip Case for Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

Best designs

The Dbrand Grip Case for Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra comes in a diverse variety of designs, including ones that glow in the dark! You can choose from magnetic and non-magnetic options of this grippy case that has scored sides.

Spigen Air Fit Pro MagFit for Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

Best cheap

Almost every single Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra case costs about $100 or so, but not the $30 Spigen Air Fit Pro MagFit. This slim, tough case is magnetic, comes in two green and black, and has thickly raised protective edges, especially around the camera unit.

TORRAS Q3 VegSkin Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case

Best all-inclusive

The TORRAS Q3 VegSkin Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case has a really classy, vegan leather finish. The camera has raised edges for drop protection, there's a spinning magnetic ring stand, and two glass screen protectors are provided in the box.

  • Back to the top ^

Incredible Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases exist, but most of them are priced around the $100 mark

From cases that glow in the dark and have grippy sides to cases that have kickstands and really powerful magnets, the best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases don't fall short of our expectations. They do, however, almost all cost an arm and a leg to acquire. Fortunately, brands like Spigen exist, making affordable Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases priced around $30 instead of $100.

If you want to stick with the cheapest option, you will be pleasantly surprised by how well it functions. The Spigen Air Fit Pro Magfit comes in two colors, black and green, and it is slim enough to allow wireless charging. It still adds military-grade drop-proofing and has incredibly strong N55 magnets inside. You also get dependable hinge protection, all for a really reasonable price.

When money isn't an object, however, you can close your eyes and pick any of the wonderful Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra cases that the most premium brands have to offer. Thinborne is my go-to brand in that case, as the brand uses aerospace-grade 600D aramid fiber to build its cases. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Case comes in three colors, black, red, and blue, and is magnetic. Measuring just 0.04 inches thin, and weighing just 0.8 ounces, you'll forget it's on your Z Fold 8 Ultra. Of course, it doesn't add rugged drop-proofing, but my colleague actually dropped his phone on the tiled marble floor with the Thinborne case on and was shocked at how it was unscathed. So if you have about $90 to spare, you too can enjoy this marvelous case on your Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra.

media:content.media:description: Looking at the reduced display crease on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8
media:content.media:text: Looking at the reduced display crease on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8
media:content.media:title: Looking at the reduced display crease on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8

item.content:encoded:
The rumored Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide turned out to just be called the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8, now with much wider screens. This square-shaped foldable has two very lovely displays, both of which could use the help of the best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 screen protectors to reinforce drop proofing. One slip of the hand and your very expensive book-style foldable could shatter badly, so don't take risk. Whether its durable glass you prefer or flexible plastic, these screen guards have it all.

These Galaxy Z Fold 8 screen protectors are the very best of what's available

Ferilinso 3 Pack Inside Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 with Back Camera Lens Protectors

Best overall

The Ferilinso 3 Pack Inside Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 is a steal. For just $20, you get a trio of military-grade 9H tempered glass front screen protectors, flexible films for the inner screen, glass camera lens guards, and two frames.
ZAGG InvisibleShield Glass XTR5 for Galaxy Z Fold 8

Best premium

The ZAGG InvisibleShield Glass XTR5 is made using unique, proprietary technologies that make it 11 times stronger than regular screen protectors and filters blue light emissions. It comes with a lifetime replacement warranty and screen repair guarantee.

Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit Optik Pro for Galaxy Z Fold 8

Best camera lens guard

Spigen's Glas.tR EZ Fit Optik Pro for the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 includes two pieces of robust tempered glass camera lens protectors. The kit also has cleaning tools and a fantastic installation frame, one that automatically aligns the protector perfectly.

Diacube Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Paperfeel Anti-Glare Matte Screen Protector

Best matte

Need an anti-reflective Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 screen protector? The Diacube Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Paperfeel Anti-Glare Matte Screen Protector is perfect for you. It has two sets of three self-healing paper-like films, three for per screen.

Ringke Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Screen Protector

Easiest installation

Ringke promises a failsafe, perfect alignment with its Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Screen Protector two-pack. The Korean accessory maker's scratch-proof 9H tempered glass protector has an anti-fingerprint oleophobic coating and a case-friendly design.

Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit Pro for Galaxy Z Fold 8

Best case-friendly

The Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit Pro two-pack of Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 screen protectors features two case-friendly 9H tempered glass screen protectors that add decent impact absorption to the Z Fold 8's external screen.

  • Back to the top ^

Picking the right Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 screen protector made easy

The best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 screen protectors come with user-friendly installation frames, multiple pieces of screen guards, and cleaning tools to make your life easy. It's not necessary to change the internal foldable display's screen protector, so you don't have to prioritize it, but it sure helps to have a spare ready to go just in case.

For an all-inclusive package that leaves nothing out without bankrupting you, the Ferilinso 3 Pack Inside Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 with Back Camera Lens Protectors is the best option. This $20 purchase will buy you a trio of 9H tempered glass screen protectors with military-grade shatter-proofing for your Fold 8's cover display. The kit also includes three tempered glass camera lens protectors and three flexible films for the Fold's internal display. Ferilinso provides two installation frames, one for each display.

If you don't like glossy finishes, then you can opt for a matte, paper-like screen protector instead. The Diacube Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Paperfeel Anti-Glare Matte Screen Protector three-pack includes three screen guards for each of the Fold's dual displays. All six matte films are anti-reflective and able to self-heal from minor abrasions. Diacube provides one installation frame and a cleaning kit as well, and the entire set is priced below $20.

Namerah is based out of Malaysia, where she lives with her four-legged life partner, a black cat named Bruce Wayne. Her hobbies include casual gaming, attempting to pet anything classified as an animal, chugging coffee, traveling profusely, and going on culinary adventures whenever possible.</p>

media:content.media:description: New Galaxy AI and Gemini Task Automation features on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra
media:content.media:text: New Galaxy AI and Gemini Task Automation features on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra
media:content.media:title: New Galaxy AI and Gemini Task Automation features on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

item.content:encoded:
The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is the true successor to the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7, with its similar aspect ratios, screen sizes, and triple camera array. As with any smartphone, it is best practice to always reinforce the display with a sturdy screen protectors. And because foldables are more fragile, with moving parts and dual screens, the need for the best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra screen protectors is even more important.

Protectors to prevent screen shatters, cracks, and abrasions

Ferilinso 3 Pack Inside Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra with Back and Camera Lens Protectors

Best overall

Get everything you need in one comprehensive Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra screen protector kit: three 9H tempered glass units for the front, three flexible films for the inside, and three more glass camera lens guards, as well as cleaning and installation tools.

BENKS GlassWarrior AR Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

Best upgrade pick

The BENKS GlassWarrior AR Screen Protector set includes a 9H tempered glass protector for the Fold 8 Ultra with an oleophobic layer to repel dirt, fingerprints, and liquids. This highly durable protector comes with a frame and cleaning kit.

Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit Optik Pro for Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

Best lens protector

The Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit Optik Pro for the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is a tempered glass camera lens protector set with an installation tool, cleaning tools, and two units of the protector. It's not the cheapest, but the quality is definitely worth it.

Milomdoi 6 in 1 Privacy Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

Best privacy

The Milomdoi 6 in 1 Privacy Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is a very popular kit because of its affordability and decent quality. This is the privacy version that comes with two frames, two lens guards, and four screen protectors.

TOCOL Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Privacy Screen Protector

Best privacy for front screen

Don't want the Milomdoi 6-in-1 kit because you don't need so many screen guards? No problem. Just get the two-pack TOCOL Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Privacy Screen Protector with two 9H+ military-grade shatter-proof tempered glass protectors.

Diacube 3+3 Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Paperfeel Anti-Glare Matte Screen Protector

Best matte

The Diacube 3+3 Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra Paperfeel Anti-Glare Matte Screen Protector includes three pieces per display, each of which has a matte, anti-glare finish that tried to imitate a paper-like feel and has self-healing properties.

  • Back to the top ^

A buyer's guide to picking the right Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra screen protectors

The best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra screen protectors will differ from person to person. Some folks like to be minimal with their shopping, opting for a single screen protector for the front. Others like to get multi-packs and have plenty of extras to spare in the future. It's also important to remember that Samsung does not recommend users to replace the internal folding screen's factory-installed screen guard at home, and not until it starts peeling off, which also factors into your decision.

Regardless of how you want to go about choosing a screen protector for your Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, I suggest getting an all-inclusive kit with multiple spare. It makes more sense to get it done with initially, so you don't have to repurchase later, and you're always prepared in case of emergencies. Ferilinso has a fantastic package that checks those boxes, giving you three 9H tempered glass screen protectors for the 6.5-inch front display, three flexible plastic films for the internal 8-inch display, and three more glass screen protectors for the camera lenses. Ferilinso also includes cleaning tools like wipes and a squeegee, as well as two installation frames.

If you want to stick with a front screen protector alone, there are several great options in different price segments. The BENKS GlassWarrior AR Screen Protector is a high-quality aluminum-lithium 9H tempered glass with an anti-reflective finish and case-friendly curved edges. It repels fingerprints, smudges, dust, and any other solid or liquid particles. Although it's on the pricey side, I have used and found the BENKS tempered glass to be of dependable quality.

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 screen protectors
item.description: The best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 screen protectors add an extra layer of crack-proofing to your shiny new foldable.
item.author: namerah.saud-fatmi@futurenet.com (Namerah Saud Fatmi)
item.dc:creator: Namerah Saud Fatmi
item.dc:description: <p>Namerah Saud Fatmi has been interested in technology ever since the days of the OG Razr Flip and back when the PlayStation Portable was the sickest console to have ever existed. Having fiddled with gadgets, PC parts, and techy tidbits since her early tweens, Namerah started sharing her expertise and opinions with the world in late 2018. Fast forward to today and she has grown into an even more curious — and seasoned — tech enthusiast with a penchant for experimenting with accessories, phones, and mobile devices. Her coverage as a Senior Editor at Android Central focuses on the world of mobile phone accessories, occasionally branching out into sects like gaming, software, hardware, and more. Before becoming a full-time writer for Android Central, Namerah was a freelance writer with bylines at other respectable publications such as XDA Developers, Neowin, and Good e-Reader. She also helps out Android Central's sister brands Windows Central and iMore on and off. Namerah is based out of Malaysia, where she lives with her four-legged life partner, a black cat named Bruce Wayne. Her hobbies include casual gaming, attempting to pet anything classified as an animal, chugging coffee, traveling profusely, and going on culinary adventures whenever possible.</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's camera
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's camera
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's camera

item.content:encoded:
Investing in the best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 screen protectors is like having an extra life in a video game; if you screw up, you can always start over with max health. Dropping your expensive new Flip 8 on a hard surface will definitely shatter the display. And if it's unfolded, both the screens could crack in one fell swoop. Why take the risk? Just get a sturdy tempered glass screen protector for the cover display and a flexible film for the internal foldable display, and you'll have an insurance policy to rely on.

To that end, here are all the best Galaxy Z Flip 8 screen protectors that you can choose. I have also included camera lens protectors, so you can reinforce them as well.

These are your best screen protector options for the Galaxy Z Flip 8

Ferilinso 3 Pack Inside Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 with 9H Tempered Glass Back and Camera Lens Protectors

Best overall

Ferilinso includes three pieces of front, inner, and camera lens protectors in this exhaustive kit, including cleaning, alignment, and installation tools. The non-folding front screen guard is made of 9H tempered glass with military-grade shatter-proofing.

Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit for Galaxy Z Flip 8

Best installation

The Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit two-pack of 9H tempered glass screen protectors for the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 comes with one of the best installation frames. It aligns automatically, includes a comprehensive cleaning kit, and has a spare to go.
ZAGG InvisibleShield Glass XTR5 for Galaxy Z Flip 8

Best premium

The InvisibleShield Glass XTR5 for Galaxy Z Flip 8 is ZAGG's most advanced screen protector yet, using graphene-infused Hexiom Impact Technology for enhanced durability and Eyesafe CPF60 blue light filters. You get a lifetime replacement warranty.

Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit Optik Pro for Galaxy Z Flip 8

Best for camera lens

Don't forget about the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's camera lenses while you're at it. This two-pack includes two pieces of full coverage camera lens protectors made of the same shatter-proof tempered glass as the Spigen Glas.tR EZ Fit for the front screen.

Natbok 2+2 Pack Soft Flexible TPU Film Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8

Best budget-friendly

The under-$10 Natbok 2+2 Pack Soft Flexible TPU Film Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is the cheapest set of decent screen guards for the Flip 8. You get an assistive installation tool in addition to two inner and two outer screen protectors.

Ferilinso 2 Pack Inside Privacy Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 with Back and Camera Lens Protectors

Best privacy

Ferilinso also offers a darkened version of its two-pack of Galaxy Z Flip 8 screen protectors for the more privacy focused individuals. Apart from two sets of privacy screen guards for both displays, it also has one set of dual camera lens protectors.

  • Back to the top ^

How to select the best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 screen protector

The best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 screen protectors are shatter-proof, fit well, come with installation frames, and have at least one spare set in the box. Fortunately, most of them are quite reasonably priced, apart from the most premium of the lot.

Ferilinso is an affordable, well-liked brand that consistently puts out good quality screen protectors for all the best flagship smartphones. The Ferilinso 3 Pack Inside Screen Protector for Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 with 9H Tempered Glass Back and Camera Lens Protectors is one of the most comprehensive kits available for the Z Flip 8. You get everything you need: three military-grade shatter-proof 9H tempered glass screen protectors for the cover display, three flexible films for the internal screen, and three sturdy camera lens protectors made of tempered glass as well. All the screen guards have an oleophobic nano-coating on top, so they repel solids and liquids alike, including dirt particles, oil residue, and fingerprints. The set even has an alignment tool for perfect at-home installation. All of that for just $15 is an outstanding deal!

If you to opt for more premium brands, Spigen and ZAGG are excellent options. Spigen is more affordable, giving you two front screen protectors made of tempered glass and an easy-to-use frame at $17.

ZAGG, on the other hand, is in a class of its own with its, with its premium-grade InvisibleShield Glass XTR5 made of graphene-infused Hexiom Impact Technology and Eyesafe CPF60 technology that filters blue light emissions. Spending $59.99 on a single front screen protector might sound insane, but it comes with a lifetime replacement warranty and a screen repair guarantee covering up to $250 worth of repairs. Since Samsung does not recommend replacing your Z Flip 8's internal screen protector, it would make sense to just get something for the external display.

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases
item.description: The best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases are available in a wide assortment of finishes, price ranges, and varying degrees of drop protection.
