media:content.media:description: Samsung's official Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases
media:content.media:text: Samsung's official Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases
media:content.media:title: Samsung's official Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases

item.content:encoded:
The cat's out of the bag: the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide is, in fact, just called the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8. The book-style foldable has a very wide aspect ration. With the help of the best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases, you can make it easier to grip and prop up, and add robust impact-proofing too.

Whether you're looking for something magnetic or a heavy-duty case that can withstand any amount of battering, it's all available. All the very best cases in every category are listed right here.

The best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases are all right here
Thinborne Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide Case

Best overall

The fantastic Thinborne case now comes in three colors. Built with aerospace-grade 600D aramid fiber, this magnetic Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 case only measures 0.04 inches thin and weighs just 0.8 ounces light.
PITAKA Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Edge Case

Best functionality

PITAKA's Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Edge Case comes in the brand's signature woven pattern. Available in four colors, this sleek case features magnetic support, raised edges around the camera unit, and a tempered glass screen protector.

FNTCASE Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Translucent Matte Case

Best affordable

Not interested in spending around $100 on a phone case? The FNTCASE Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Translucent Matte Case costs below $30, but it still adds hinge protection, a magnetic ring, a screen protector, and military-grade drop-proofing.

SUPCASE UB Grip Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Case with Stand

Best rugged

SUPCASE has a massive fan following for good reason. The brand's rugged UB Grip case is tougher than nails, adding military-grade impact resistance, shock absorption, and bonuses like a stand, built-in screen guard, ring holder, and hinge protection.

VRS DESIGN Terra Guard Modern for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8

Best simple

Jazzy phone cases aren't for everyone. The slim but tough VRS DESIGN Terra Guard Modern for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 keeps it simple. This plain, black case has a soft matte finish, comes with a screen protector, and supports magnetic accessories.

Poetic Spartan for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Case

Best clear

The Poetic Spartan for Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Case is a scratch and drop resistant case that has a clear back and raised bezels all around. It also has a magnetic ring embedded in the back and a kickstand tucked away inside the reinforced hinge.

  • Back to the top ^

Choose a Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases that matches your needs and fits your budget

There are hundreds of Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases out there, but choosing the right one means identifying your needs and then matching that with something reliable. I have been testing cases, screen protectors, and accessories for many years, which is why you can rely on my expertise when hunting for the best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 cases.

Thinborne is a Texas-based brand that has a fantastic reputation. The company isn't very big, but they are stringent with their quality control. This is why the Thinborne Galaxy Z Fold 8 Wide Case is easily the ultimate case for your wide-screened Z Fold 8. The only complaint you will ever have is its price, because its high cost is the only drawback to choosing this case. It is exceedingly thin, has strong magnets inside, and weighs barely anything. Thinborne added two more colors, so the case now comes in black, red, and blue. If you can afford it, this is the optimal Galaxy Z Fold 8 case.

Now if you want to set your sights to a lower price bracket, there are still plenty of decent options with reasonable drop-proofing and handy add-ons like a magnetic ring. The FNTCASE Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Translucent Matte Case only costs $27, but that pricing isn't indicative of the quality. I have tested many cases from the brand and found them to perform really well. It comes in pink, blue, and black, and you can also get a clear variant but that has a glossy finish. FNTCASE promises military-grade shock-proofing, and the Fold 8's spine has been thickly reinforced.

item.title: T-Mobile is already giving away the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 for FREE — how to claim yours
item.description: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 preorder deals are live, and T-Mobile is already giving away the foldable phone for free with eligible trade-in and new line.
item.author: patrick.farmer@futurenet.com (Patrick Farmer)
item.dc:creator: Patrick Farmer
item.dc:description: <p>Patrick (he/him) is the eCommerce Editor of Android Central. After working in independent bookstores for the better part of a decade and using freelance writing as a side hustle, Patrick switched to writing full time in 2020. Patrick’s writing has appeared in the Chicago Tribune, The Hill, BestReviews, OddityMall, and many other publications across the web. He joined Android Central as the Deals and Commerce Writer in 2022 and now runs the site’s eCommerce efforts and coverage of major sale events. Although he’s written about everything from exercise equipment to turntables, Patrick’s work nowadays is primarily concerned with saving people money on tech. He’s particularly passionate about eliminating buyer’s remorse and taking subjects that can be confusing for shoppers — such as switching wireless carriers or buying a new smart TV — and breaking them down into simple, easy-to-understand terms. Whether you’re a veteran tech-head or a novice, Patrick offers honest, no-nonsense shopping advice with years of hands-on experience to back it up. When he isn’t hunting down the best discounts or digging through the fine print of a carrier deal, Patrick enjoys trekking around the Rocky Mountains, camping out at a brewery, or spending time with his beloved polydactyl cat. &nbsp;</p>

media:content.media:description: Galaxy Z Fold 8 cameras
media:content.media:text: Galaxy Z Fold 8 cameras
media:content.media:title: Galaxy Z Fold 8 cameras

item.content:encoded:
The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 was unveiled just moments ago, but companies like T-Mobile have wasted no time in dropping some exceptional preorder deals ahead of the August 7th release date. Add a line with the Experience Beyond plan and trade in an old or broken phone and the Big Three carrier will hook you up with enough promo credits to make the new Z Fold 8 completely free! 

Sure, T-Mobile's Experience Beyond may not be the cheapest unlimited plan around, but it's nothing that $1,900 of potential savings can't fix. If you're interested in one of the most innovative phones of 2026 and you were planning to update your wireless service anyway, this deal is worth grabbing ASAP. 

T-Mobile will already give you the Galaxy Z Fold 8 for FREE with new deal

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 256GB: $1,899.99 FREE with eligible trade-in and new line at T-Mobile

Trade in an old or broken phone and add a line with the Experience Beyond plan and T-Mobile will give you up to $1,900 off the new Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8, enough to make the innovative foldable phone completely free!

Don't have anything to trade in? Skip that part of the process altogether and you'll still enjoy $1,100 off the phone with any Experience plan, or you can save $900 by adding a line with "most plans" on T-Mobile's site. View Deal

Samsung just unveiled the Galaxy Z Fold 8 (alongside the Z Fold 8 Ultra and Z Flip 8) during a Galaxy Unpacked event earlier today, and the phones are already turning heads. For starters, it's hard to describe the Z Fold 8 as a true successor to last year's Galaxy Z Fold 7, as Samsung has given the phone a shorter and wider, "passport-style" design.

This new look makes the phone easy to pocket and use with one hand while folded, while unfolding the device gives you an experience akin to an Android tablet. And as usual, the foldable phone's two AMOLED display are absolutely stunning and smooth as butter. Under the hood, you still get the powerful Z Fold experience that we've all come to know and love, with a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset, 12GB of RAM, and all of the latest AI-boosted software tricks. The phone does clock in at $1,899, however, so deals like the T-Mobile promotion above are essential.  

What about the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra?

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra 256GB: $2,099.99 From $200 with trade-in and new line at T-Mobile

If you prefer the narrower form factor of last year's Z Fold 7, the new Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is the answer, coming complete with an ultra-thin design, upgraded 5,000mAh battery, and the powerful Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chip. It's also quite expensive, which is why T-Mobile is offering up to $1,900 off the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra when you trade in and add a line with the Experience Beyond plan.

Don't have anything to trade in? Just like the Z Fold 8, T-Mobile will give you $1,100 off the Z Fold 8 Ultra when you add a line with an Experience plan on its website. Other plans will still secure you $900 off, so take your pick. View Deal

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's camera
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's camera
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8's camera

item.content:encoded:
It's official: the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 has arrived, and it doesn't disappoint. One problem, however, is that the innovative foldable now boasts a starting price of $1,199.99 for the 256GB configuration, which is $100 more expensive than its predecessor.

Fortunately, I'm already hard at work tracking down the best Galaxy Z Flip 8 preorder deals so you don't have to go searching. We haven't seen too many offers just yet, but you can already make this premium flip phone considerably cheaper if you play your cards right. Keep reading for my recommendations, and don't forget to check back later to see what's new: I'll keep adding new offers to this list as I find them.

Quick links

  • Samsung Unpacked LIVE: latest news and everything that was announced
  • The best Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Fold 8 Ultra deals
  • T-Mobile: Get the Z Flip 8 for $100 with eligible plan
  • Samsung: up to $600 off with trade-in, free credit
  • Verizon: TBA
  • AT&T: up to $1,000 off with trade-in, new line
  • Amazon: free $200 gift card with preorder
  • Best Buy: free $200 gift card with preorder

Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 deals

T-Mobile: Get the Z Flip 8 for just $100 with eligible unlimited plan

You read that right. Add a line with an eligible unlimited plan and T-Mobile will give you the new Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 for just 100 bucks, no trade-in required!View Deal

Verizon:View Deal

AT&T: just 5.56/month with eligible trade-in, new line

Add a line with an eligible data plan and send in an old or broken device and AT&T will give you up to $1,000 off the new Galaxy Z Flip 8.View Deal

Samsung: up to $600 off the Galaxy Z Flip 8 with trade-in | $150 off

Trade in an old or broken device and Samsung will give you up to $600 off your Z Flip 8 preorder. Alternatively, you could skip the trade-in process altogether and secure yourself a $150 discount. View Deal

Amazon: FREE $200 gift card with preorder

Order the Z Flip 8 unlocked from Amazon and you'll get a free $200 gift card, no trade-in required!View Deal

Best Buy: FREE $200 gift card with preorder

Just like Amazon, Best Buy will give you a free $200 gift card when you preorder the unlocked Galaxy Z Flip 8 on its site. View Deal

FAQ

When does the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 come out?

Alongside the book-style Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 is scheduled for release on August 7th. This gives you just a few weeks to take advantage of the preorder offers detailed above.

How much does the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 cost?

The Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 will start at $1,199.99 / €1,299.00 / £1,149.00 for the 256GB configuration of the phone. That's $100 more expensive than last year's Galaxy Z Flip 7, which was already more expensive than its predecessor. This price increase is even further proof that no one is exempt from the ongoing RAM crisis, which is why finding good deals is so important nowadays.

I already own the Galaxy Z Flip 7. Is it worth upgrading?

The Galaxy Z Flip 8 and last year's Galaxy Z Flip 7 share a lot of similarities on the design and hardware side, especially at first glance. Both flip phones come with 12GB of RAM and a 4,300mAh battery, and they feature virtually identical camera systems and displays, although the Z Flip 8 does clock in at 8 grams lighter than its predecessor. The Z Flip 8 also uses the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 processor, which is a bit more powerful than the last generation's Exynos chip.

Do these minor upgrades justify the $100 price increase? It's a tough call, but if you're already happy with your Galaxy Z Flip 7, I see no reason to rush to the store to grab the new phone. On the other hand, if you're a flip phone fanatic, the Z Flip 8 offers one of the best experiences around.

item.title: Which Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 color should you buy?
item.description: The book-shaped Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 comes in three standard colors, plus a bonus online exclusive. Here's how to choose which one you should buy.
item.author: namerah.saud-fatmi@futurenet.com (Namerah Saud Fatmi)
item.dc:creator: Namerah Saud Fatmi
item.dc:description: <p>Namerah Saud Fatmi has been interested in technology ever since the days of the OG Razr Flip and back when the PlayStation Portable was the sickest console to have ever existed. Having fiddled with gadgets, PC parts, and techy tidbits since her early tweens, Namerah started sharing her expertise and opinions with the world in late 2018. media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and 8 Ultra
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and 8 Ultra
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and 8 Ultra

item.content:encoded:
The boxy Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 is available in three standard colors, plus an online exclusive, totaling up to four shades overall. As trying as it can be to try to choose only one shade to buy from four different colors, it can also be a good thing. You might come across a shade that you didn't even know you'd love!

Without further ado, let me help you decide which Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 colorway you should buy. To start things off, let us walk through the colors. Familiarize yourself with what your choices are, then you can identify your winner.

A walk through all the colors of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 in Lavender

Lovely lavender

Lavender is a stylish look on the wide, square-shaped Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8. This pale, cool-toned colorway accentuates the book-like appearance of the foldable.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 in Graphite

Can't go wrong

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 in Graphite is the safest pick. If you're a fickle-minded person who changes their mind easily, this is a classic ashy black hue that you won't easily regret.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 in Cream

Vanilla latte aura

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 in Cream is the color of milk in a vanilla latte: a pale, creamy, warm shade of milky white. It is the most minimalist-friendly color of the lot.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 in Pistachio

Internet only

The Pistachio colorway of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 is the successor to the last-gen Z Fold 7's Mint finish. This green colorway is an online exclusive too, but it is a lot deeper than last year's Mint and more warm-toned.

  • Back to the top ^

Buying the right Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 colorway: A helpful guide

Trying to decide which Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 color should you buy? Now that you've had a good look at all the colorways the Fold 8 comes in, the decision must be easier to make. Of the four Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 colorways, two are basic safe choices. Graphite is the classic black option that almost always outsells all other shades, because most folks don't want bold colors. Cream is another such color, though white isn't as widely preferred as black.

I encourage you to take a risk and choose a color that speaks to you, however unusual it may be! The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 in Lavender is immediately recognizable as the Fold 8, plus it looks divine. The wide, boxy cut of the device is enhanced and accentuated in this light purple hue.

Exclusivity is an attractive feature to many. If you're gunning for something different and less widely available, the warm Pistachio colorway stands out. True to its name, Pistachio is a warm green color that is an online exclusive. If you do go for Pistachio, bear in mind that spare parts for less popular colors can be harder to acquire. So if you're a clumsy, accident prone person who doesn't use impact proof phone cases, best to err on the side of caution and stick with one of the three standard colors instead.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra vs. Galaxy Z Fold 7: A new name with better specs
item.description: The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is here as the true successor to the Galaxy Z Fold 7. Apart from the new 'Ultra' suffix, you also get a bigger battery, sharper folding screen, and an improved ultrawide camera.
item.author: roydon.cerejo@gmail.com (Roydon Cerejo)
item.dc:creator: Roydon Cerejo
item.dc:description: <p>Roydon has been writing about consumer tech for over a decade, and has reviewed a wide variety of products, from TVs and headphones, to cameras and PC components. Smartphones and laptops have been his main focus areas for the past couple of years, where he has extensively covered both spaces in terms of reviews, features, interviews, and live events. When not helping someone pick out their next phone, you’ll find him hunting for new TV shows to binge watch, and struggling to complete his backlog of PS4 games.</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

item.content:encoded:
Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

Fancy new title

The flagship Z Fold phone now gets 'Ultra' in its name. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is essentially the successor to the Galaxy Z Fold 7, with the usual expected upgrades. You get Qualcomm's latest and greatest chip, better cameras, a bigger battery, and faster charging. We're still waiting on the proper dust proofing though, and better selfie cameras.

Pros

  • Larger 5,000mAh battery
  • Supports faster 45W charging
  • Higher-resolution folding screen
  • Powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 SoC
  • Seven years of OS upgrades

Cons

  • Colors aren't very interesting
  • Still only IP48 rated
  • Selfie cameras are still 10MP
  • More expensive

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7

Samsung's most popular Fold yet

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 got a major design overhaul, making it slimmer and lighter than ever before. Despite ditching support for the S Pen and being more expensive, demand for it was higher than even Samsung anticipated.

Pros

  • The crease is practically non-existent
  • Doesn't feel like a heavy remote when folded anymore
  • Larger and wider cover screen
  • Truly a 2-in-1 device
  • Shares the same camera as the S25 Ultra
  • Excellent performance
  • That blue is oh so blue, and looks incredible

Cons

  • No S Pen support
  • Even more expensive than before
  • There's no dust protection
  • Battery life is meh

At July's Samsung Galaxy Unpacked event, the company finally unveiled it's latest line of foldables and smartwatches. The foldables are pretty much bang on in line with all the leaked images we've seen over the past few months. What I wasn't expecting was the shake up in the naming convention, which understanably, is bound to cause a bit of confusion.

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is the flagship model and will be replacing the Galaxy Z Fold 7, while the standard Galaxy Z Fold 8 is actually a cheaper and wider version of the flagship, previously dubbed as the 'Wide Edition.'

It's now been full year since the Galaxy Z Fold 7's existence and it's already officially selling for $400 lower than the launch price, and this is outside sale season. This could dip further in the months to come as Samsung tries to empty its inventory.

If you've skipped the Galaxy Z Fold 7 or have been waiting for the new Fold 8 series to launch, should you spring for the latest Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra or save a few hundred Benjamins with the older (but still great) model?

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra vs. Galaxy Z Fold 7: Pricing and availability

Our fears did come true as Samsung has raised prices by $100. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra now starts at $2,099.99 and this is for the 256GB variant. There are also 512GB and 1TB storage options, with only the latter getting 16GB of RAM. The tiers below come with 12GB of RAM. Violet Shadow is the new hero color for the Z Fold 8 Ultra, while the other two are Graphite and Cream. Samung will be bundling a six-month complementary subscription to the AI Pro plan, with every Z Fold 8 Ultra.

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 will be a year old now and is already retailing for less, starting at $1,499.99 for 256GB and going up to $2,000 for the 1TB variant. There could be a further price drop before it goes out of circulation so you should keep an eye on this if you want to save a good chunk of change. The Galaxy Z Fold 7 had one of the best colors of all the Fold phones yet and has proven to be hugely popular showing strong demand in the U.S. market.

The recent rise in component costs have caught up though as Samsung quietly bumped up the MSRPs of the higher storage variants by $80. Compared to the new Z Fold 8 Ultra though, you're still saving quite a bit.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra vs. Galaxy Z Fold 7: Design and display

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra looks pretty much identical to the Galaxy Z Fold 7, and that's fine. The Fold 7 got a major design overhaul just last year making the slimmest Fold yet. The Z Fold 8 Ultra is a tiny bit thinner at 4.1mm when unfolded, but measures the same 8.9mm when folded.

The weight of the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra has also stayed the same at 215 grams, despite Samsung using a larger battery. Physically, you really shouldn't notice too much of a difference between the two phones even if you hold them side by side.

The Galaxy Z Fold 7 scored well in terms of build quality and durabililty but it only had an IP48 rating for dust and water resistance. Sadly, this hasn't improved on the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra. The water proofing is still okay, but the phone still lacks proper dust proofing. In comparison, the Honor Magic V5 and Vivo X Fold 5 feature IP58 and IP59 dust and water resistance, while the Google Pixel 10 Pro Fold has an IP68 rating.

The displays on the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra gets some nice upgrades. The folding screen is still 8 inches, but Samsung has bumped up the resolution to 2,504x2,256. Compared to the already high resolution of 1,968x2,184 on the Galaxy Z Fold 7, the Fold 8 Ultra should offer even crisper visuals and sharper text.

The outer display on the Fold 8 Ultra has stayed pretty much the same, but it does get more durable protection in the form of Corning Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3. There were murmurs a few months back that support for the S Pen will be brought back in the Z Fold 8 Ultra, but that hasn't happened.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra vs. Galaxy Z Fold 7: Hardware and software

Samsung typically ships the Galaxy Z Fold devices with flagship chipsets, and it's done the same once again with the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra. The foldable features the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset for Galaxy devices, same as the ones used in the Galaxy S26 series.

Another big upgrade that Samsung has delivered in the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is a larger battery. We finally have a 5,000mAh capacity battery which should deliver a good battery life boost over the 4,400mAh in the Galaxy Z Fold 7.

Charging speed has also increased to 45W from the measly 25W on the previous model. I would have liked to see 60W wired charging like the Galaxy S26 Ultra but I'll take anything above 25W at this point. Qi2 charging is still not native as the Z Fold 8 Ultra lacks in-built magnets. You'll have to use a compatible case for Qi2.

Specifications

Category

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7

Operating System

One UI 9 (Android 17), seven years of OS upgrades

One UI 8 (Android 16), seven years of OS upgrades

Display

Inner: 8-inch LTPO AMOLED, 1-120Hz, crease-less, 2,504x2,256 pixels

Outer: 6.5-inch LTPO AMOLED, 120Hz, Corning Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3

Inner: 8-inch LTPO AMOLED, 120Hz, crease-less, 1,968x2,184

Outer: 6.5-inch LTPO AMOLED, 120Hz, Corning Gorilla Glass Ceramic 2

Processor

Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy

Snapdragon 8 Elite for Galaxy

RAM

12GB/16GB

12GB/16GB

Storage

256GB, 512GB, 1TB

256GB, 512GB, 1TB

Rear Cameras

200MP wide;
50MP ultra-wide;
10MP telephoto (3x zoom)

200MP wide;
12MP ultra-wide;
10MP telephoto (3x zoom)

Front Camera

10MP (internal), 10MP (external)

10MP (internal), 10MP (external)

Battery

5,000mAh

4,400mAh

Charging

45W wired, 20W wireless

25W wired, 15W wireless, Qi2 support

IP Rating

IP48

IP48

Weight

215g

215g

Dimensions

(Unfolded) 158.4 x 143.2 x 4.1 mm ; (Folded) 158.4 x 72.8 x 8.9 mm

(Unfolded) 158.4 x 143.2 x 4.2 mm ; (Folded) 158.4 x 72.8 x 8.9 mm

Colors

Violet Shadow, Graphite, Cream

Blue Shadow, Silver Shadow, Jet Black, Mint

The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra ships with One UI 9, that's based on Android 17. Samsung has been testing One UI 9 in beta for a while on S26 devices, giving users a glimpse of what to expect. Some of the new features include an upgraded Quick Panel with toggles for the brightness, sound, and media player now being independently adjustable. There's a new Text Spotlight feature that shows selected text in a larger floating window. There's plenty more AI and user interface features to unpack, so stay tuned for our detailed guide.

Many of these One UI 9 features should also make their way to the Galaxy Z Fold 7 with future updates. It's possible that some might be hardware dependent so expect a few to be missing when you update your Z Fold 7 to One UI 9.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra vs. Galaxy Z Fold 7: Cameras

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 got a 200MP main camera upgrade just last year, and the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra uses the same one. But it finally gets a higher-resolution 50MP ultrawide camera, which should bring about a tangible quality improvement. A leak had suggested that the Z Fold 8 Ultra would get a 12MP telephoto, but this hasn't turned out to be true. It's still 10MP with an optical zoom of 3X.

The selfie cameras on the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra have also gone unchanged. You still get the two 10MP sensors on the outer and inner screens. We loved the cameras on the Galaxy Z Fold 7 when we tested it so having better sensors in the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra would have made it more competitive against foldables like the OPPO Find N6.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra vs. Galaxy Z Fold 7: Which one should you buy?

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra has gotten more expensive but you're also getting some worthwhile upgrades like a newer SoC, larger battery with quicker charging, a sharper folding screen, and a better ultrawide camera. The only reason to buy this at launch is if you're getting a good trade-in deal or pre-order discounts. Otherwise, I'd wait a few months till Samsung inevitably slashes the price itself.

If you need to pull the trigger right away, you can't go wrong with the Galaxy Z Fold 7. It now retails for lower than what it launched for, it's incredibly slim and light, and you won't have any issues with performance. Keep in mind that we didn't find the battery life to be great and there's no S Pen support if that's something you think you'll need.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

The price of being the latest

The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is not a major upgrade over the Fold 7, but there are a few areas like the battery and camera where it's genuinely better.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7

Still a champ

The Galaxy Z Fold 7 is Samsung's best foldable for most users and is slightly better value now after a price drop. You're in for a slim device that's built well and has excellent global after sales support.

item.title: Which Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 color should you buy?
item.description: Can't makeup your mind about which Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 color to buy? Settle the decision once and for all with a little help from us.
item.author: namerah.saud-fatmi@futurenet.com (Namerah Saud Fatmi)
item.dc:creator: Namerah Saud Fatmi
item.dc:description: <p>Namerah Saud Fatmi has been interested in technology ever since the days of the OG Razr Flip and back when the PlayStation Portable was the sickest console to have ever existed. Having fiddled with gadgets, PC parts, and techy tidbits since her early tweens, Namerah started sharing her expertise and opinions with the world in late 2018. Fast forward to today and she has grown into an even more curious — and seasoned — tech enthusiast with a penchant for experimenting with accessories, phones, and mobile devices. Her coverage as a Senior Editor at Android Central focuses on the world of mobile phone accessories, occasionally branching out into sects like gaming, software, hardware, and more. Before becoming a full-time writer for Android Central, Namerah was a freelance writer with bylines at other respectable publications such as XDA Developers, Neowin, and Good e-Reader. She also helps out Android Central's sister brands Windows Central and iMore on and off. Namerah is based out of Malaysia, where she lives with her four-legged life partner, a black cat named Bruce Wayne. Her hobbies include casual gaming, attempting to pet anything classified as an animal, chugging coffee, traveling profusely, and going on culinary adventures whenever possible.</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8

item.content:encoded:
Samsung is offering the same colors of the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 as it did with the previous-gen Galaxy Z Flip 7 which came in four colors. This makes it easy for you to choose a color, even if one of the four shades is an online exclusive. The colors are largely "safe" options, with only two colorful shades to choose from.

Before cashing in on a single choice, take a good look at all the finishes available at launch to help you decide which Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 color should you buy.

Check out all the colors of the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8

Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 in Pink

A soft approach

Pastels have taken over the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8. The Pink colorway is a soft, pale pink with a matching pink metal frame.

Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 in Graphite

Safest pick

Black is ever-present in the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 color story. The Graphite is one we've seen time and time again, with nearly every phone ever released by the brand. It's a no-nonsense safe pick.

Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 in Cream

Neither here not there

Cream is a minimalist shade of white, following the pastel playbook of the Galaxy Z Flip 8 color story. It's not too boring, but it's not very exciting either. White stands out without being too "in your face" or bold.

Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 in Mint

Online-only option

Seems that Samsung is following last year's script with the online exclusive Mint colorway of the Z Flip 8. This fresh minty green is yet another pastel colorway, and not a new one either as the Flip 7 came in the same option.

  • Back to the top ^

Three colors, one choice: Picking the right Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 color made easy

Answering the question of which Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 color should you buy isn't too difficult. You can either stick with your safest bet, which the plain black Graphite colorway, or choose something light and pastel.

If you're not one to stick with the traditional black colorway, you'll have to be excited about lighter shades. The Z Flip 8 comes in three other shades, the last of which is online exclusive: Pink, Cream, and Mint.

Pink is a blushing rosy hue that isn't entirely new to Samsung's Flip series. Just like Mint is a recurring shade across generations, the Galaxy Z Flip 3, Flip 4, and Flip 5 all came in similar pinky shades as well. If you're feeling nostalgic, either Pink or Mint will both be great Flip 8 colors for you.

However, if you're not looking to attract too much attention without being too predictable, Cream is a good choice to consider. A pale white hue is a minimalist's dream. It is different without being overtly attention grabbing.

item.title: Which Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra color should you buy?
item.description: The much anticipated Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is here and it is glorious. Available in four colors, it's going to be a tough call choosing only one color.
item.author: namerah.saud-fatmi@futurenet.com (Namerah Saud Fatmi)

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra

item.content:encoded:
Choosing to buy the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is easy. After all, it's Samsung's largest dual-fold smartphone yet. What's hard is making a decision on which Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra color should you buy. That's why I'm here to make things easy for you. First, you need to appreciate all the different colors it comes in and then figure out which one is the right match for you.

Bathe in the glory of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra and its four shades

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra in Violet Shadow

Star of the show

Indulge in the luxury of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra in Violet Shadow, the best colorway from the Fold 8 and Flip 8 lineup. It is a dark, deep, and royal indigo. Violet Shadow is neither too bold nor in any way boring.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra in Graphite

Classic black outfit

Graphite is a staple across all of Samsung's devices, whether its foldables or non-foldables. This dark black ensemble of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is a classic option that many will favor, because you can't go wrong with black.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra in Cream

Opulent and elegant

Instead of a neutral white, the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is available in a pale, warmer tone called Cream. This minimalist shade looks very elegant, though it will require more frequent cleaning as it is so light.

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra in Green Shadow

Online exclusive

Samsung always has at least one online exclusive colorway. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra's online-only shade is Green Shadow, a smokey green with hints of forest green in it. This shade is just as attractive as Violet Shadow, unless you dislike green as a whole.

  • Back to the top ^

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra comes in a range of deep, elegant colors

I am loving the color scheme of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra. This gigantic foldable comes in a squad of deep, elegant colors. With the exception of Cream, every single shade is on the darker side. Violet Shadow is the hero, a mesmerizing shade of dark indigo that's almost black in certain lighting. It is sure to be very popular with the crowds.

I usually suggest opting for the black colorway of the phone when in doubt, but Violet Shadow is dark enough to appease even those who would usually choose Graphite to be safe. Why play it safe when you can get the signature new color of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra?

Meanwhile, if dark indigo isn't your jam, Green Shadow is a charming shade to consider. I know a lot of people have very strong opinions about green, but trust me on this one. Green Shadow is a smokey, forest green hue that exudes class and sophistication. It's not asking for attention, but it's easy on the eyes nonetheless.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025: This is the upgrade you want
item.description: The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 finally delivers the upgrades we've been waiting for. The smartwatch has a thinner design and a new squircle case, much bigger 800mAh battery, better durability, and a new Qualcomm silicon that's outstanding.
item.author: harish.jonnalagadda@futurenet.com (Harish Jonnalagadda)
item.dc:creator: Harish Jonnalagadda
item.dc:description: <p>Harish Jonnalagadda is Android Central's Senior Editor overseeing the mobile category. He started his tech journey at a very young age by tweaking Windows XP installations, and that hobby ignited an enthusiasm that led him into the world of PC modding. After picking up a Bachelor's degree in computer science, he decided to pursue his passion by covering PC hardware at VR-Zone, where he reviewed motherboards, video cards, and DRAM modules.</p><p><br>While he used iPhones initially, switching to the Nexus 4 served as the catalyst to explore Android's burgeoning ecosystem, and he pivoted to writing about mobile tech, joining Android Central's newsroom back in 2014. As a Senior Editor, he oversees the site's coverage of Chinese phone brands, enthusiast audio products, networking hardware, and storage servers, leveraging his engineering background and extensive experience testing mobile hardware to evaluate the latest phones and accessories.<br></p><p>In over a decade of covering Android, he has used pretty much every single major phone released globally, giving him an unrivaled view into Google's mobile platform. His specialty is Chinese brands; he's charted the growth of all the major Chinese manufacturers from their inception, and he uses that knowledge to share unique insights. When not testing the latest gadgets, he can be found reading sci-fi novels on his e-reader, and occasionally yelling at the TV in frustration as Arsenal squander yet another title run.</p>

media:content.media:description: Comparing the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 on a hands-on event
media:content.media:text: Comparing the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 on a hands-on event
media:content.media:title: Comparing the Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 on a hands-on event

item.content:encoded:
Samsung
Galaxy Watch Ultra 2

The definitive smartwatch

With the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, Samsung has delivered a ton of upgrades. The biggest is the OLED panel, which now goes up to 5,000 nits. You also get a thinner titanium case with a unique squircle design, much bigger battery, and IP69K ingress protection. The best part is that the Watch Ultra 2 isn't any heavier in spite of all the bigger battery.

Samsung
Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025

Still good - with a caveat

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025 is identical to the 2024 model, and the only difference is the increase in storage — that's it. Samsung didn't make any changes between the two, and as a result, it's chunky, doesn't have as big a battery, and misses out on the new Qualcomm silicon. If you don't mind the omissions and are able to get it on sale, the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025 is still a good choice, but I'd suggest getting the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 instead.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025: Pricing and availability

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 made its debut at Samsung's Galaxy Unpacked launch event in London on July 22, 2026, and it is now on sale globally. The smartwatch retails for $699, and that's pretty decent when you consider you're getting 64GB of storage and a ton of new features.

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025 launched at $649, the same as its predecessor. Which is understandable as the smartwatch didn't actually bring any new features, and other than double the amount of onboard storage, it was identical to the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2024. Thankfully, that's not the case this year.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025: Design and features

Samsung is changing the design of the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 a little bit; the smartwatch has a squircle case that's made out of titanium, and the design looks a lot cleaner than the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025. Samsung is offering the smartwatch in Titanium Silver and Titanium Gray, and we'll need to wait and see if both models are available in all global regions.

What I like the most is that the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 is significantly thinner at 10mm (versus 12.1mm); this was a big pain point with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2024 and 2025 models, and it's good to see Samsung addressing it. Another change has to do with durability; the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 gets IP69K ingress protection, giving it added resilience against dust and water ingress.

You still get a massive 47mm case, and I like that Samsung didn't change this. The display is slightly bigger at 1.52 inches thanks to thinner bezels, and the resolution is also a little higher at 498x498 (Ultra 2025 has 480x480). But where the Watch Ultra 2 stands out the most is the overall brightness; Samsung is somehow touting brightness of up to 5,000 nits, and that's a significant boost from the 3,000-nit Watch Ultra 2025.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025: Hardware and specs

If I had to point to a single upgrade that makes the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 truly stand out, it would be the battery. The smartwatch comes with an 800mAh battery, and this is an area where Samsung absolutely did the right thing. The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025's 590mAh battery is decent enough, but the much bigger battery should allow the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 to last even longer.

Powering the smartwatch is the latest Snapdragon Wear Elite, and this again is a big change; last year's model had the Exynos W1000, and the switch to Qualcomm should deliver better efficiency, boosting battery life even more. The smartwatch gets 2GB of memory and 64GB of storage as standard, and it retains similar connectivity as last year's model, including NFC.

Category

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025

Display

47mm 1.52-inch AMOLED, 498x498, 5,000 nits max

47mm 1.5-inch AMOLED, 480x480, 3,000 nits max

OS

Wear OS 7 Powered by Samsung, One UI 9 Watch

Wear OS 5, Powered by Samsung, One UI 8 Watch

Chipset

Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite, 3nm

Samsung Exynos W1000, 3nm

Memory & Storage

2GB/64GB

2GB/32GB, 2GB/64GB

Connectivity

Bluetooth + LTE, Wi-Fi, NFC, dual-band GPS

Bluetooth + LTE, Wi-Fi, NFC, dual-band GPS

Durability

10ATM, IP69K water resistance, MIL-STD-810H durability

10ATM, IP68 water resistance, MIL-STD-810H durability

Battery

800mAh, 100% charge in 90 min (40% in 30 min)

590mAh, 100% charge in 120 min

Dimensions

47.4 x 47.1 x 10mm

47.4 x 47.4 x 12.1mm

Weight

61.5g

60.5g

Colors

Titanium Silver, Titanium Gray

Titanium Sliver, Titanium Gray, Titanium White, Titanium Blue

Sensors

Samsung BioActive, SpO2, heart rate sensor, accelerometer, gyro, barometer, altimeter

Samsung BioActive, SpO2, heart rate sensor, accelerometer, gyro, barometer, altimeter

Coming to fitness and health monitoring, the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 gets Samsung's BioActive Sensor, and it includes all the hardware needed to monitor vitals and daily activity. You get Samsung's Wear OS 7 out of the box with One UI 9 Watch, and there's a lot to like with the software in general. Samsung is the way to go if you want the best Android smartwatch, and the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 continues that momentum on the software front.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025: Which should you buy?

There's no doubt in my mind: if you're ready to upgrade to a bigger smartwatch, you need to buy the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. It has exciting upgrades across the board, and the thinner design combined with a bigger battery and brighter AMOLED panel make it a much better choice.

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025 is still a decent choice if you're able to get a sizeable discount, but of the two, I'd suggest getting the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 — it's just a better all-round smartwatch.

Samsung
Galaxy Watch Ultra 2

The definitive smartwatch

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 comes with all the upgrades that matter. The bigger battery combined with better internals should allow it to last a lot longer, it gets much brighter, and the design itself is thinner and much more durable.

Samsung
Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025

Still good - with a caveat

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2025 is still a great smartwatch, but it doesn't quite measure up against the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. But if you're able to get a decent discount, the smartwatch is worthy of consideration.

item.title: Samsung's new Galaxy devices double down on Snapdragon-powered AI
item.description: Samsung and Qualcomm are going all-in, supercharging the new Galaxy foldables, watches, and smart glasses with next-gen Snapdragon chips.
item.author: techkritiko@gmail.com (Jay Bonggolto)
item.dc:creator: Jay Bonggolto
item.dc:description: <p>Jay Bonggolto always keeps a nose for news. He has been writing about consumer tech and apps for as long as he can remember, and he has used a variety of Android phones since falling in love with Jelly Bean. When he's not writing, he likes to spend time outside, stealing scenes with his phone camera. Send him a direct message via X or LinkedIn.</p>

media:content.media:description: Galaxy phones powered by Snapdragon silicon
media:content.media:text: Galaxy phones powered by Snapdragon silicon
media:content.media:title: Galaxy phones powered by Snapdragon silicon

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Qualcomm now powers Samsung's entire AI ecosystem, expanding beyond foldables into Galaxy smartwatches and upcoming AI smart glasses.
  • The Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy promises faster on-device AI, stronger gaming performance, smarter cameras, and better power efficiency to Samsung's latest foldables.
  • Samsung's first Snapdragon-powered smartwatches debut with the Galaxy Watch 9 and Watch Ultra 2, using Snapdragon Wear Elite for more personalized health tracking and AI experiences.

Samsung’s new Galaxy launch was more than just foldables. Qualcomm also used the occasion to show just how much deeper its partnership with Samsung has become, with Snapdragon chips now powering the company's newest phones, smartwatches, and its upcoming AI-powered smart glasses.

The biggest upgrade is for Samsung’s flagship foldables. Qualcomm’s new Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy powers the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold 8, and, in select markets, the Galaxy Z Flip 8. Qualcomm says the custom chip blends its Oryon CPU, next-gen NPU, and upgraded GPU for faster on-device AI, better gaming performance, smarter camera features, and improved power efficiency.

And the collaboration isn’t limited to smartphones. Samsung’s wearables lineup is also seeing a significant shakeup, with the Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 set to be the company’s first smartwatches to run on Qualcomm’s Snapdragon Wear Elite platform.

Qualcomm says the platform was built around “Personal AI” that employs advanced sensor fusion to better understand user activity throughout the day, providing more proactive health insights while maintaining strong connectivity and performance.

Perhaps the most interesting expansion is outside of phones and watches. Qualcomm, Samsung, and Google are expanding their Android XR partnership to include intelligent eyewear with the Snapdragon AR1 Gen 1 platform. Samsung already unveiled new smart glasses designs with Gentle Monster and Warby Parker, with larger collections slated for market later this fall.

Qualcomm says the platform is meant to enable more contextual AI experiences in lightweight glasses as opposed to bulky headsets.

Android Central's Take

If Snapdragon can actually deliver seamless AI across your phone, watch, and future smart glasses, you’ll be greeted with experiences that feel genuinely connected, rather than a bunch of isolated features. That said, we’ve heard enough of these “AI ecosystem” promises before, and not all of them have lived up to the hype. The real test will be Samsung’s ability to make those features useful enough that people actually notice them outside of launch-day demos.

item.title: Best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 cases
item.description: Your hunt for the best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 cases ends here, for I have curated the perfect list of worthwhile contenders.
item.author: namerah.saud-fatmi@futurenet.com (Namerah Saud Fatmi)
item.dc:creator: Namerah Saud Fatmi
item.dc:description: <p>Namerah Saud Fatmi has been interested in technology ever since the days of the OG Razr Flip and back when the PlayStation Portable was the sickest console to have ever existed. Having fiddled with gadgets, PC parts, and techy tidbits since her early tweens, Namerah started sharing her expertise and opinions with the world in late 2018. Fast forward to today and she has grown into an even more curious — and seasoned — tech enthusiast with a penchant for experimenting with accessories, phones, and mobile devices. Her coverage as a Senior Editor at Android Central focuses on the world of mobile phone accessories, occasionally branching out into sects like gaming, software, hardware, and more. Before becoming a full-time writer for Android Central, Namerah was a freelance writer with bylines at other respectable publications such as XDA Developers, Neowin, and Good e-Reader. She also helps out Android Central's sister brands Windows Central and iMore on and off. Namerah is based out of Malaysia, where she lives with her four-legged life partner, a black cat named Bruce Wayne. Her hobbies include casual gaming, attempting to pet anything classified as an animal, chugging coffee, traveling profusely, and going on culinary adventures whenever possible.</p>

media:content.media:description: Thinborne Galaxy Z Flip 8 Aramid Fiber Magnetic Case
media:content.media:text: Thinborne Galaxy Z Flip 8 Aramid Fiber Magnetic Case
media:content.media:title: Thinborne Galaxy Z Flip 8 Aramid Fiber Magnetic Case

item.content:encoded:
The Flip 8 is slimmer and lighter than ever, so be sure to grab one or two of the best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 cases. You don't want to take away from the thinness or lightness of such a compact foldable, so be sure to grab a sturdy, scratch-proof phone case that isn't bulky or heavy. I have rounded up the best options available right here for your perusal, be it hinge protection you're looking for or extras like magnetic Qi2 support.

Protective phone cases for your Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8
Thinborne Galaxy Z Flip 8 Aramid Fiber Magnetic Case

Best overall

Thinborne continues to be the hero of the people with its fantastic, impossibly thin Aramid Fiber Magnetic Case for the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8. Equipped with a magnetic ring, it supports all Qi2 accessories, even wireless charging ones.

Spigen Ultra Hybrid Pro MagFit for Galaxy Z Flip 8 in Neo One

Coolest design

Spigen Ultra Hybrid Pro MagFit for Galaxy Z Flip 8 in Neo One sports the coolest design ever, plus it supports magnetic accessories. It's a see-through case with an x-ray of the insides of the Flip 8 sitting on the back.

Cresee Magnetic Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 Case

Best clear

Cresee Magnetic Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 Case is super affordable and thin TPU case with a hard back. It adds scratch protection and a magnetic ring to your Flip 8, but don't expect robust drop-proofing from this lightweight clear case.

FNTCASE Galaxy Z Flip 8 Case 360 Spin Magnetic Stand

Best built-in grip

The FNTCASE Galaxy Z Flip 8 Case 360 Spin Magnetic Stand features thickly reinforced hinge protection and a magnetic ring-shaped stand that rotates 360 degrees. Because the built-in ring stand has N52 magnets inside, you can still use magnetic accessories.
PITAKA Galaxy Z Flip 8 Back Case

Best camera protection

The PITAKA Galaxy Z Flip 8 Back Case features a woven, aramid fiber build with an upgraded camera protection design. The raised bezels around the lenses don't bulk up the Flip 8 as much because of the ultra-thin design of the case.

Poetic Spartan for Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8

Best heavy-duty

Available in one see-through and two opaque colors, the Poetic Spartan for Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 features 20ft military-grade drop protection. It is a magnetic case with a stand built in the reinforced hinge, built-in screen protection, and a magnetic ring.

Spigen Nano Pop MagFit for Galaxy Z Flip 8

Best color combo

The Spigen Nano Pop MagFit for Galaxy Z Flip 8 holds a cult classic status. The blue and yellow Blueberry Navy colorway in particular is extremely popular. The Nano Pop MagFit case for the Flip 8 is slim, impact-proof, and magnetic, all for a reasonable price.

  • Back to the top ^

Breaking down how to pick the right case for your Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8

There are hundreds of phone cases out there, but only a few of them qualify as the best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 cases. A stalwart case for a foldable needs to allow for uninterrupted folding and unfolding, and it can't be too thick either. People buy foldables like the Flip 8 for the lightness and thinness, after all, and a thick case takes that away.

Thinborne continues to be the gift that keeps on giving. This Texas-based brand always delivers on every vital front: slimness, lightness, and handiness. The Thinborne Galaxy Z Flip 8 Aramid Fiber Magnetic Case is a marvel, measuring just 0.04 inches thin and weighing just 15.9 grams. It is almost like having a Dbrand skin on instead of a phone case. What's harder to believe is that Thinborne somehow embedded powerful magnets inside this aerospace-grade 600D aramid fiber case. The ultra-premium build does have one unfortunate consequence: it makes the case insanely expensive.

If you don't want to spend $80 on the Thinborne case, you still have plenty of excellent Z Flip 8 cases to choose from. However, none of them get as thin as the Thinborne case.

Spigen is one of the top smartphone case manufacturers in the world. For the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8, I recommend two of Spigen's classic models: the Ultra Hybrid Pro MagFit and Nano Pop MagFit. Both are slim, magnetic, and add reasonable shock absorption, and they are priced affordably as well. If you want to be able to admire the color of your Galaxy Z Flip 8, I recommend the see-through Spigen Ultra Hybrid Pro MagFit.

item.title: How to preorder the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Fold 8 Ultra — I tracked down the best early deals on the web (so far)
item.description: We just got our first look at the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Fold 8 Ultra, and I'm already hard at work tracking down the best preorder deals so you don't have to.
item.author: patrick.farmer@futurenet.com (Patrick Farmer)
item.dc:creator: Patrick Farmer
item.dc:description: <p>Patrick (he/him) is the eCommerce Editor of Android Central. After working in independent bookstores for the better part of a decade and using freelance writing as a side hustle, Patrick switched to writing full time in 2020. Patrick’s writing has appeared in the Chicago Tribune, The Hill, BestReviews, OddityMall, and many other publications across the web. He joined Android Central as the Deals and Commerce Writer in 2022 and now runs the site’s eCommerce efforts and coverage of major sale events. Although he’s written about everything from exercise equipment to turntables, Patrick’s work nowadays is primarily concerned with saving people money on tech. He’s particularly passionate about eliminating buyer’s remorse and taking subjects that can be confusing for shoppers — such as switching wireless carriers or buying a new smart TV — and breaking them down into simple, easy-to-understand terms. Whether you’re a veteran tech-head or a novice, Patrick offers honest, no-nonsense shopping advice with years of hands-on experience to back it up. When he isn’t hunting down the best discounts or digging through the fine print of a carrier deal, Patrick enjoys trekking around the Rocky Mountains, camping out at a brewery, or spending time with his beloved polydactyl cat. &nbsp;</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and 8 Ultra
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and 8 Ultra
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 and 8 Ultra

item.content:encoded:
The big day has arrived, and the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 preorder deals are trickling in. Whether you want the ultra-wide Z Fold 8 or the super-powered Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra, there are already a few ways to save money on these premium foldables before they hit store shelves on August 7th. Considering that the devices start at a steep $1,899, I'll take just about any offer I can get.

Unlike some phone releases that feel purely perfunctory, Samsung is really changing things up with its newest book-style foldable phones. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 sports a brand new form factor that we've compared to a passport, while the brand new Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is one of the largest foldables we've ever seen.

These phones aren't just pretty to look at, however. They also feature incredible specs under the hood, with powerful Snapdragon chips, useful AI-boosted software features, and years of guaranteed OS updates. As usual, they're also pretty expensive, so I've dropped all of the web's best Z Fold 8 preorder deals into this guide. Didn't find anything yet? Check back later: we're sure to see more offers go live in the coming days.

Quick links

  • Samsung Unpacked LIVE: latest news and everything that was announced
  • The best Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 8 preorder deals
  • T-Mobile: FREE Galaxy Z Fold 8 with eligible trade-in, new line
  • Verizon: new line savings, trade-in deals
  • Samsung: up to $1,200 of trade-in credit
  • Amazon: free $350 gift card with purchase
  • Best Buy: trade-in savings, free gift cards
  • AT&T: FREE Z Fold 8 with eligible trade-in, new line

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 deals

T-Mobile: Get a FREE Z Fold 8 with new line and eligible trade-in

The phones aren't even out yet and T-Mobile will already give you the Galaxy Z Fold 8 for FREE when you add a line and process a qualified trade-in on the carrier's site. View Deal

Verizon: Add a line with the Simplicity plan and get the Z Fold 8 for $30 per month

Add a line with Verizon's new Simplicity plan and you'll get the new Galaxy Z Fold 8 for only $30 per month for 48 months. No trade-in required! Alternatively, you could process a trade-in and add a line with the Unlimited Ultimate plan and score up to $1,200 off the phone. View Deal

AT&T: get a FREE Z Fold 8 with eligible trade-in, new line

Trade in an old or broken phone and add a line with a qualified unlimited plan and AT&T might hook you up with a FREE Galaxy Z Fold 8. View Deal

Samsung: up to $1,200 off with trade-in | $200 off without

Trade in an old or broken phone and Samsung will give you up to $1,200 off the Galaxy Z Fold 8 during the preorder period. Don't have anything to trade in? Skip that part of the process and you'll still score a $200 credit when you buy unlocked.View Deal

Amazon: get a FREE $350 gift card with unlocked phone purchase

Buy the Galaxy Z Fold 8 unlocked from Amazon and you'll get a free $350 gift card with your preorder! There's also some additional trade-in savings up for grabs (in the form of an additional gift card).View Deal

Best Buy: free $200 gift card with preorder, trade-in savings

Purchase the Galaxy Z Fold 8 before its August 7th release date and Best Buy will hook you up with a free $200 gift card. There's also some trade-in credit up for grabs if you're looking to ditch an old phone. View Deal

Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra deals

T-Mobile: Score $1,900 off with eligible trade-in, new line

Similar to the Z Fold 8 deal described above, T-Mobile will slash $1,900 off the price of the Z Fold 8 Ultra when you add a line with an eligible unlimited plan and trade in an old or broken device. View Deal

Verizon: Score up to $1,200 off the Z Fold 8 Ultra with eligible trade-in and new line

Add a line with Verizon's Unlimited Ultimate plan and you'll score up to $1,200 off the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra when you trade in an old or broken phone.View Deal

AT&T: up to $1,900 off with eligible trade-in, new line

Add a line with an eligible data plan and AT&T will give you up to $1,900 of trade-in credit, which could put a serious dent into the price of the Z Fold 8 Ultra. View Deal

Samsung: up to $1,200 off with trade-in | $200 off without

Buy the Z Fold 8 Ultra directly from the source and you'll be eligible for up to $1,200 of trade-in credit, no activation required. If you don't have anything trade in, you'll still get a $200 credit with your purchase, plus some bundle discounts when you pair the phone with Samsung accessories.View Deal

Best Buy:View Deal

Amazon: get a FREE $350 gift card with Z Fold 8 Ultra preorder

Just like the Z Fold 8, Amazon will give you a free $350 gift card when you preorder the unlocked Z Fold 8 Ultra on its site, no strings attached. View Deal

FAQ

How much does the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 cost?

The new wide-style Galaxy Z Fold 8 is set to cost $1,899.99 for the 256GB version, while the Fold 8 Ultra will start at $2,099 USD. Samsung's foldable phones have always been expensive, but the ongoing RAM crisis has contributed to increased prices across the board. It's just one more reason why the above preorder deals are so essential.

When does the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 come out?

The Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8 lineup is set to officially hit store shelves on August 7th, which gives you just over two weeks to take advantage of the deals above.

How does the Galaxy Z Fold 8 compare to the Z Fold 8 Ultra?

It may sound strange, but the Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra is the true successor to last year's Galaxy Z Fold 7. Starting at $2,099, the phone boasts virtually identical dimensions to last year's foldable with an upgraded 5,000mAh battery, Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset, and 12GB of RAM for the 256GB and 512GB models. It also weighs just 215 grams, which is the same as last year's model. The fact that Samsung was able to pack a larger battery into the same thin frame as the Galaxy Z Fold 7 is impressive, but some folks may be disappointed that the design is otherwise largely unchanged.

The Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8, on the other hand, is where things start to look interesting. The phone boasts a 7.6-inch main display and 5.5-inch cover display (both stunning AMOLED, of course), resulting in a device that looks a bit like a passport book. Clocking in at just 201 grams, the Z Fold 8 also comes with the same Snapdragon chip as the Ultra, plus a 4,800mAh battery, the latest AI-boosted software features, and up to 16GB of RAM. Moreover, the Z Fold 8 is $200 cheaper than the Ultra.

Is the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8 worth it?

It depends! If you already own a foldable phone, such as the Z Fold 7 or the Motorola Razr Fold, there's really no reason to upgrade right away. These OEMs push out new foldable phones every year, so it's okay (and probably financially responsible) for you to skip a generation here and there.

On the other hand, if you want to try the latest in foldable phone technology and you're able to get a good deal that works with your budget, the Z Fold 8 and Z Fold 8 Ultra really do offer some of the most innovative Android experiences around.

item.title: Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 still skips the S Pen, and that's getting harder to ignore
item.description: Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 8 drops S Pen support, betting everything on AI-powered productivity.
item.author: techkritiko@gmail.com (Jay Bonggolto)
item.dc:creator: Jay Bonggolto
item.dc:description: <p>Jay Bonggolto always keeps a nose for news. He has been writing about consumer tech and apps for as long as he can remember, and he has used a variety of Android phones since falling in love with Jelly Bean. When he's not writing, he likes to spend time outside, stealing scenes with his phone camera. Send him a direct message via X or LinkedIn.</p>

media:content.media:description: Looking at the reduced display crease on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8
media:content.media:text: Looking at the reduced display crease on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8
media:content.media:title: Looking at the reduced display crease on the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 8

item.content:encoded:
What you need to know

  • Samsung quietly abandoned stylus support for both the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Fold 8 Ultra, just like the Z Fold 7.
  • The missing S Pen leaves a productivity gap, removing a tool that made handwritten notes, document annotation, and sketching easy on the Fold's large inner display.
  • Marketing the Fold 8 Ultra as the ultimate productivity machine while removing the very hardware tool that made previous generations so effective sends mixed signals to users.

Samsung just launched its Galaxy Z Fold 8 series, but one longtime Fold feature is absent. Samsung has quietly ditched S Pen support from the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Fold 8 Ultra (again) after years of marketing the Fold as a productivity-first device, with no mention of stylus support anywhere in its official announcement, spec sheets, or otherwise.

This is despite Samsung doubling down on AI-powered productivity. The new Fold line-up is all about multitasking with Galaxy AI, Gemini Intelligence, split-screen workflows, and larger displays leading the way.

Additionally, the Fold 8 Ultra is billed as the company’s ultimate productivity machine, combining an 8-inch display with a Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 for Galaxy chip, a 5,000mAh battery, and a 200MP main camera. The standard Fold 8 has a lighter 201g design, a 4,800mAh battery, and dual 50MP cameras but retains the same flagship chipset.

What’s missing is any mention of the S Pen that used to help separate Samsung’s book-style foldables from the competition. In previous iterations of the Fold (except the Z Fold 7), the stylus was a peripheral, giving users a way to handwrite notes, annotate documents, and scribble on the large inner display.

This decision is even more surprising given that the new Fold lineup is designed to help users do more with AI and features like Gemini Notebook, drag-and-drop multitasking, and app automation across more than 40 apps and services supported. Those upgrades double down on the Fold’s productivity focus, but they also make the lack of pen input feel like an even bigger compromise.

Android Central's Take

Samsung seems to be giving off mixed signals here. You can’t market the Fold 8 as the ultimate productivity phone and then quietly take away one of the tools that actually made it feel productive. Sure, AI can summarize meetings and organize notes, but it can’t replace the precision of putting pen to paper. But if Samsung wants Fold users to embrace an AI-first future, it also has to remember that sometimes the smartest feature isn’t AI at all — it’s letting people write things down.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch 9: It's an easy choice
item.description: The Galaxy Watch 9 has a few upgrades, but it's pretty similar to last year. The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, on the other hand, has upgrades across the board. Interested in buying a Samsung smartwatch this year? Read this.
item.author: harish.jonnalagadda@futurenet.com (Harish Jonnalagadda)
item.dc:creator: Harish Jonnalagadda
item.dc:description: <p>Harish Jonnalagadda is Android Central's Senior Editor overseeing the mobile category. He started his tech journey at a very young age by tweaking Windows XP installations, and that hobby ignited an enthusiasm that led him into the world of PC modding. After picking up a Bachelor's degree in computer science, he decided to pursue his passion by covering PC hardware at VR-Zone, where he reviewed motherboards, video cards, and DRAM modules.</p><p><br>While he used iPhones initially, switching to the Nexus 4 served as the catalyst to explore Android's burgeoning ecosystem, and he pivoted to writing about mobile tech, joining Android Central's newsroom back in 2014. As a Senior Editor, he oversees the site's coverage of Chinese phone brands, enthusiast audio products, networking hardware, and storage servers, leveraging his engineering background and extensive experience testing mobile hardware to evaluate the latest phones and accessories.<br></p><p>In over a decade of covering Android, he has used pretty much every single major phone released globally, giving him an unrivaled view into Google's mobile platform. His specialty is Chinese brands; he's charted the growth of all the major Chinese manufacturers from their inception, and he uses that knowledge to share unique insights. When not testing the latest gadgets, he can be found reading sci-fi novels on his e-reader, and occasionally yelling at the TV in frustration as Arsenal squander yet another title run.</p>

media:content.media:description: Comparing the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 on a hands-on event
media:content.media:text: Comparing the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 on a hands-on event
media:content.media:title: Comparing the Samsung Galaxy Watch 9 with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 on a hands-on event

item.content:encoded:
Samsung
Galaxy Watch Ultra 2

Living up to its name

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 has all the upgrades you want in 2026. It has a bigger battery, brighter AMOLED, faster internals, and it is somehow thinner than its predecessor. The 47mm case is on the bigger end of the scale, but it is made out of titanium, and looks exquisite. If you're ready to switch to a bigger smartwatch, the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 is the obvious choice.

Samsung
Galaxy Watch 9

A great all-rounder

The Galaxy Watch 9 isn't too different to what we got last year, but the biggest positive is that it uses the same Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite platform as the Ultra 2. You get a slightly bigger battery, and while the overall design hasn't changed — it's still available in 44mm and 40mm variants — it is just as thin and light as the Galaxy Watch 8. Samsung is instead focusing heavily on the software, rolling out a new health dashboard and fitness metrics — which you get on the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 as well.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch 9: Pricing and availability

Samsung unveiled the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 and Galaxy Watch 9 at a launch event in London on June 22, 2026. Both smartwatches are now on pre-order globally, with sales starting shortly.

Like previous years, Samsung has two models of the Galaxy Watch 9: 40mm and 40mm. The 40mm model with Bluetooth costs $379, and if you need LTE, you'll need to pay $429. The 44mm Bluetooth variant is $409, and the LTE model is $459. There's a modest increase from last year, but the Galaxy Watch 9 is still an affordable choice.

The Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, on the other hand, retails for $699, a $50 increase. You are getting an extensive feature-set, bigger battery, and titanium chassis, and that's reflected in the cost.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch 9: Design and display

The Galaxy Watch 9 looks identical to its predecessor, and that's a good thing. You still get a smartwatch that looks great, and it's just as thin (8.6mm) and light (34g) as the Galaxy Watch 8.

Samsung didn't change its choice of materials either, so you get an aluminum case with a plastic housing on the underside to accommodate the BioActive module. Like last year, the Galaxy Watch 9 is sold in 40mm and 44mm variants, and Samsung is even selling the same color models. There's no rotating bezel on either device, but you get side-mounted buttons.

If you're looking to get a watch that looks different to last year, you'll need to pick up the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. It has a significantly thinner design that looks much better this time, and the 47mm case gives it a dominating presence that you just don't get with the 44mm Galaxy Watch 9. It's made out of titanium, and has better durability as well — 10ATM and IP69K versus 5ATM and IP68 of the Galaxy Watch 9.

Coming to the display, the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 gets a 1.52-inch AMOLED that goes up to a ridiculous 5,000 nits. The 44mm Galaxy Watch 9 has the same 1.47-inch panel as last year, and the 40mm model retains the 1.34-inch screen, with both going up to 3,000 nits. Outside of that, you don't see much of a difference between the two smartwatches.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch 9: Features and specs

What's good to see is that both smartwatches use the Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite. Ahead of launch, it was unclear if the Galaxy Watch 9 would come with last year's Exynos W100 instead of the new Qualcomm silicon, and Samsung did the right thing by using Qualcomm across the board.

Category

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2

Samsung Galaxy Watch 9

Display

47mm: 1.52-inch AMOLED, 498x498, 5,000 nits max

44mm: 1.47-inch AMOLED, 480x480, 3,000 nits max

40mm: 1.34-inch AMOLED, 438x438, 3,000 nits max

OS

Wear OS 7 Powered by Samsung, One UI 9 Watch

Wear OS 7 Powered by Samsung, One UI 9 Watch

Chipset

Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite, 3nm

Qualcomm Snapdragon Wear Elite, 3nm

Memory & Storage

2GB/64GB

2GB/32GB

Connectivity

Bluetooth + LTE, Wi-Fi, NFC, dual-band GPS

Bluetooth + LTE, Wi-Fi, NFC, dual-band GPS

Durability

10ATM, IP69K water resistance, MIL-STD-810H durability

5ATM, IP68 water resistance, MIL-STD-810H durability

Battery

800mAh, 100% in 90 min (40% in 30 min)

44mm : 445mAh, 100% in 90 min (45% in 30 min)

40mm: 390mAh, 100% in 80 min (50% in 30 min)

Dimensions

47.4 x 47.1 x 10mm

44mm: 46.0 x 43.7x 8.6mm

40mm: 42.7 x 40.4x 8.6mm

Weight

61.5g

44mm: 34.0g

40mm: 31.5g

Colors

Titanium Silver, Titanium Gray

44mm: Silver, Grey

40mm: Cream, Grey

Sensors

Samsung BioActive, SpO2, heart rate sensor, accelerometer, gyro, barometer, altimeter

Samsung BioActive, SpO2, heart rate sensor, accelerometer, gyro, barometer, altimeter

While the hardware itself is exciting, Samsung turned its attention to the software this year, and both smartwatches have a ton of new health and fitness features. There's Energy Tracking, Sleep Apnea Detection, a new running-focused feature, and Vitals, a dashboard that gives you a broad overview of your vitals in one area.

You also get a new Nutrition Alert feature alongside Trail Run that measures detailed elevation changes and climbing progress. And as for the software, both smartwatches run the latest Wear OS 7 with Samsung's One UI 9 Watch. Samsung does a great job with the software in general, and One UI 9 Watch has the best feature-set of any Android smartwatch.

Samsung Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 vs. Galaxy Watch 9: Which should you buy?

Choosing between the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 and Galaxy Watch 9 is entirely down to what you need in a smartwatch. If you need a smaller watch that nails the basics and looks stylish, just get the Galaxy Watch 9. You're not missing out on the essentials, and this is a proven design that has worked really well for Samsung in the last two years.

But if you want a bigger smartwatch that grabs attention, you should get the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2. Yes, it costs a lot more, but you also get a bigger screen that gets much brighter, much bigger battery that lasts nearly twice as long, better durability, and a titanium design that looks exquisite. If I had to choose a model between these two, I'm getting the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 anytime — it's much more exciting.

Samsung
Galaxy Watch Ultra 2

Living up to its name

Samsung outdid itself with the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, delivering what is possibly one of the best smartwatches ever made.

Samsung
Galaxy Watch 9

A great all-rounder

The Galaxy Watch 9 may not have as many upgrades as the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, but it still nails the basics. You're getting a stylish smartwatch with standout software and health monitoring features, and it doesn't cost as much.

item.title: I went hands-on with Samsung's new game-changing AI smartglasses and Samsung is committed to thwarting public opinion of them
item.description: Samsung finally gave us a hands-on look at its upcoming Android smart glasses while addressing the elephant in the room - public opinion of smart glasses.
item.author: derrek.lee@futurenet.com (Derrek Lee)
item.dc:creator: Derrek Lee
item.dc:description: <p>Derrek has had a long-time interest in mobile technology since the age of 12, which came out of his love for Nokia phones, particularly models like the Nokia N90. Since then, he closely followed the evolution of tech and the transition of Nokia from Symbian to Windows Phone, which eventually led him to Android with phones like the Moto X Pure Edition and LG G5. Since 2013, Derrek has dipped in and out of tech journalism as nothing more than a passion project while receiving his Bachelor of Arts degree in Film &amp; Digital Media at the University of California, Santa Cruz. That is until 2020, when he became the news editor for Android Central, guiding the team's coverage of Google, Android, Wear OS, and related topics. Now, as managing editor, Derrek guides the site's editorial content and direction, helping the team reach and resonate with readers, old and new, who are just as passionate about tech as we are.</p> <p>Throughout his time at Android Central, he has reviewed many products, from phones to smartwatches, smart scales, and even smart rings, establishing himself as an expert with these devices. As a fitness enthusiast, Derrek always looks for ways to incorporate tech into his life by closely examining the intersection of fitness and tech. He is also very passionate about tech being financially accessible, which often drives his decision-making in the types of products he tests out and what he recommends.</p>

media:content.media:description: Hands-on with Samsung Glasses with Warby Parker and Gentle Monster styles
media:content.media:text: Hands-on with Samsung Glasses with Warby Parker and Gentle Monster styles
media:content.media:title: Hands-on with Samsung Glasses with Warby Parker and Gentle Monster styles

item.content:encoded:
Samsung is set to fully launch its "Intelligent Eyewear" this fall, and at Unpacked, the company gave us a look at its designs from Warby Parker and Gentle Monster. I've already played with prototype Android XR glasses a few times, but this is my first time getting hands-on time with production design models, and I feel like it's finally starting to come together.

However, given how long it's taking Google and Samsung to bring these to market, I'm wondering if this new effort can survive the hurdles of a shifting public opinion on smart glasses.

AI meets style

The models I got to handle were not functioning products, but just something to give us a peek at what we're getting ahead of the actual launch. So while I couldn't actually use the glasses or even put them on my face, I at least got to hold them and get a feel for them, and I was quite impressed.

Of the new designs I got to see, the Gentle Monster model caught my eye, as I prefer its more classic style, which is closer to the kinds of glasses I wear. However, the Warby Parker glasses looked more stylish, so I'm sure there will still be plenty of appeal for these.

This is why Samsung's partnerships with Gentle Monster and Warby Parker are so important, as highlighted by James Choi, corporate EVP and head of XR R&D at Samsung.
